text.KJV: 8:20 Behold, God will not cast away a perfect man, neither will hee helpe the euill doers:
text.NABRE: Behold, God will not cast away the upright; neither will he take the hand of the wicked.

text.NRSV: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with shouts of joy.
text.Rotherham: At length he shall fill with laughter thy mouth, and thy lips, with a shout of triumph:
text.KJV: 8:21 Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with reioycing.
text.NABRE: Once more will he fill your mouth with laughter and your lips with rejoicing.

text.NRSV: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”
text.Rotherham: They who hate thee, shall be clothed with shame, but, the tent of the lawless, shall not be!
text.KJV: 8:22 They that hate thee shall be cloathed with shame, and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.
text.NABRE: Those who hate you shall be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked shall be no more.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said—
text.KJV: 9:1 Then Iob answered, and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Job’s Second Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Indeed I know that this is so; but how can a mortal be just before God?
text.Rotherham: Of a truth, I know that so it is, But how can a mortal be just with GOD?
text.KJV: 9:2 I know it is so of a trueth: but howe should man be iust with God.
text.NABRE: I know well that it is so; but how can anyone be in the right before God?

text.NRSV: If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand.
text.Rotherham: If he choose to contend with him, he cannot answer him, one of a thousand:
text.KJV: 9:3 If he will contend with him, he cannot answere him one of a thousand.
text.NABRE: Should one wish to contend with him, he could not answer him once in a thousand times.

text.NRSV: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength—who has resisted him, and succeeded?—
text.Rotherham: Wise in heart, and alert in vigour, What man hath hardened himself against him, and prospered!
text.KJV: 9:4 He is wise in heart, and mightie in strength: who hath hardened himselfe against him, and hath prospered?
text.NABRE: God is wise in heart and mighty in strength; who has withstood him and remained whole?

text.NRSV: he who removes mountains, and they do not know it, when he overturns them in his anger;
text.Rotherham: Who removeth mountains, unawares, Who overturneth them in his anger;
text.KJV: 9:5 Which remoueth the mountains, and they know not: which ouerturneth them in his anger:
text.NABRE: He removes the mountains before they know it; he overturns them in his anger.

text.NRSV: who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;
text.Rotherham: Who shaketh the earth, out of its place, and, the pillars thereof, shudder;
text.KJV: 9:6 Which shaketh the earth out of her place, & the pillars thereof tremble:
text.NABRE: He shakes the earth out of its place, and the pillars beneath it tremble.

text.NRSV: who commands the sun, and it does not rise; who seals up the stars;
text.Rotherham: Who commandeth the sun, and it breaketh not forth, and, about the stars, he putteth a seal;
text.KJV: 9:7 Which commandeth the Sunne, and it riseth not: and sealeth vp the starres.
text.NABRE: He commands the sun, and it does not rise; he seals up the stars.

text.NRSV: who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the Sea;
text.Rotherham: Who spreadeth out fire heavens, by himself alone! and marcheth along, on the heights of the sea;
text.KJV: 9:8 Which alone spreadeth out the heauens, and treadeth vpon the waues of the Sea.
text.NABRE: He alone stretches out the heavens and treads upon the back of the sea.

text.NRSV: who made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the chambers of the south;
text.Rotherham: Who made the Bear, the Giant and the Cluster, and the chambers of the south;
text.KJV: 9:9 Which maketh Arcturus, Orion and Pleiades, and the chambers of the South.
text.NABRE: He made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the constellations of the south;

text.NRSV: who does great things beyond understanding, and marvelous things without number.
text.Rotherham: Who doeth great things, past finding out, and marvels, beyond number.
text.KJV: 9:10 Which doeth great things past finding out, yea and wonders without number.
text.NABRE: He does things great and unsearchable, things marvelous and innumerable.

text.NRSV: Look, he passes by me, and I do not see him; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.
text.Rotherham: Lo! he cometh upon me, yet can I not see him, Yea he passeth on, yet can I not discern him.
text.KJV: 9:11 Loe, hee goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceiue him not.
text.NABRE: Should he come near me, I do not see him; should he pass by, I am not aware of him;

text.NRSV: He snatches away; who can stop him? Who will say to him, ‘What are you doing?’
text.Rotherham: Lo! he snatcheth away, who can bring it back? Who shall say unto him, What wouldst thou do?
text.KJV: 9:12 Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say vnto him, What doest thou?
text.NABRE: Should he seize me forcibly, who can resist? Who can say to him, “What are you doing?”

text.NRSV: “God will not turn back his anger; the helpers of Rahab bowed beneath him.
text.Rotherham: As for GOD, if he withdraw not his anger, under him, will have submitted themselves—the proud helpers.
text.KJV: 9:13 If God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers doe stoupe vnder him.
text.NABRE: He is God and he does not relent; the helpers of Rahab bow beneath him.

text.NRSV: How then can I answer him, choosing my words with him?
text.Rotherham: How much less that, I, should answer him, should choose my words with him?
text.KJV: 9:14 How much lesse shall I answere him, and choose out my words to reason with him?
text.NABRE: How then could I give him any answer, or choose out arguments against him!

text.NRSV: Though I am innocent, I cannot answer him; I must appeal for mercy to my accuser.
text.Rotherham: Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, to be absolved, I would make supplication.
text.KJV: 9:15 Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answere, but I would make supplication to my Iudge.
text.NABRE: Even though I were right, I could not answer, but should rather beg for what was due me.

text.NRSV: If I summoned him and he answered me, I do not believe that he would listen to my voice.
text.Rotherham: Though I had called, and he had answered me, I could not believe, that he would lend an ear to my voice.
text.KJV: 9:16 If I had called, and had answered me, yet would I not beleeue that he had hearkened vnto my voice:
text.NABRE: If I appealed to him and he answered me, I could not believe that he would listen to me;

text.NRSV: For he crushes me with a tempest, and multiplies my wounds without cause;
text.Rotherham: For, with a tempest, would he fall upon me, and would multiply my wounds without need;
text.KJV: 9:17 For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.
text.NABRE: With a storm he might overwhelm me, and multiply my wounds for nothing;

text.NRSV: he will not let me get my breath, but fills me with bitterness.
text.Rotherham: He would not suffer me to recover my breath, for he would surfeit me with bitter things.
text.KJV: 9:18 Hee will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitternesse.
text.NABRE: He would not allow me to draw breath, but might fill me with bitter griefs.

text.NRSV: If it is a contest of strength, he is the strong one! If it is a matter of justice, who can summon him?
text.Rotherham: If it regardeth vigour, bold is he! If justice, who could summon him?
text.KJV: 9:19 If I speake of strength, loe, hee is strong: and if of iudgement, who shall set me a time to pleade?
text.NABRE: If it be a question of strength, he is mighty; or of judgment, who will call him to account?

text.NRSV: Though I am innocent, my own mouth would condemn me; though I am blameless, he would prove me perverse.
text.Rotherham: If I should justify myself, mine own mouth, would condemn me,—I blameless? then had it shewn me perverse.
text.KJV: 9:20 If I iustifie my selfe, mine owne mouth shall condemne me: If I say, I am perfect, it shall also prooue me peruerse.
text.NABRE: Though I were right, my own mouth might condemn me; were I innocent, it might put me in the wrong.

text.NRSV: I am blameless; I do not know myself; I loathe my life.
text.Rotherham: I blameless? I should not know my own soul, I should despise my own life!
text.KJV: 9:21 Though I were perfect, yet would I not know my soule: I would despise my life.
text.NABRE: I am innocent, but I cannot know it; I despise my life.

text.NRSV: It is all one; therefore I say, he destroys both the blameless and the wicked.
text.Rotherham: One thing, there is, for which cause, I have said it, The blameless and the lawless, he bringeth to an end.
text.KJV: 9:22 This is one thing, therefore I said it; he destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
text.NABRE: It is all one! therefore I say: Both the innocent and the wicked he destroys.

text.NRSV: When disaster brings sudden death, he mocks at the calamity of the innocent.
text.Rotherham: If, a scourge, slay suddenly, at the despair of innocent ones, he mocketh.
text.KJV: 9:23 If the scourge slay suddenly, hee will laugh at the triall of the innocent.
text.NABRE: When the scourge slays suddenly, he scoffs at the despair of the innocent.

text.NRSV: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the eyes of its judges—if it is not he, who then is it?
text.Rotherham: The earth, hath been given into the hand of a lawless one, The faces of her judges, he covereth, If not, then who is it?
text.KJV: 9:24 The earth is giuen into the hand of the wicked: he couereth the faces of the Iudges thereof; if not, where, and who is hee?
text.NABRE: The earth is given into the hands of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges. If it is not he, who then is it?

text.NRSV: “My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away, they see no good.
text.Rotherham: My days, therefore, are swifter than a runner, They have fled, they have seen no good.
text.KJV: 9:25 Now my dayes are swifter then a Poste: they flee away, they see no good.
text.NABRE: My days are swifter than a runner, they flee away; they see no happiness;

text.NRSV: They go by like skiffs of reed, like an eagle swooping on the prey.
text.Rotherham: They have passed away with boats of paper-reed, like a vulture [which] rusheth upon food.
text.KJV: 9:26 They are passed away as the ships: as the Eagle that hasteth to the pray.
text.NABRE: They shoot by like skiffs of reed, like an eagle swooping upon its prey.

text.NRSV: If I say, ‘I will forget my complaint; I will put off my sad countenance and be cheerful,’
text.Rotherham: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will lay aside my sad countenance, and brighten up,
text.KJV: 9:27 If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leaue off my heauinesse, and comfort my selfe.
text.NABRE: If I say: I will forget my complaining, I will lay aside my sadness and be of good cheer,

text.NRSV: I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
text.Rotherham: I am afraid of all my pains, I know, that thou wilt not pronounce me innocent.
text.KJV: 9:28 I am afraid of all my sorrowes, I know that thou wilt not holde me innocent.
text.NABRE: Then I am in dread of all my pains; I know that you will not hold me innocent.

text.NRSV: I shall be condemned; why then do I labor in vain?
text.Rotherham: I, shall be held guilty,—Wherefore then, in vain, should I toil?
text.KJV: 9:29 If I be wicked, why then labour I in vaine?
text.NABRE: It is I who will be accounted guilty; why then should I strive in vain?

text.NRSV: If I wash myself with soap and cleanse my hands with lye,
text.Rotherham: Though I bathe myself in snow water, and cleanse, in cleanness itself, my hands,
text.KJV: 9:30 If I wash my selfe with snow water, and make my handes neuer so cleane:
text.NABRE: If I should wash myself with soap and cleanse my hands with lye,

text.NRSV: yet you will plunge me into filth, and my own clothes will abhor me.
text.Rotherham: Then, in a ditch, wouldst thou plunge me, and mine own clothes should abhor me:
text.KJV: 9:31 Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine owne clothes shall abhorre me.
text.NABRE: Yet you would plunge me in the ditch, so that my garments would abhor me.

text.NRSV: For he is not a mortal, as I am, that I might answer him, that we should come to trial together.
text.Rotherham: For he is not a man like myself, whom I might answer, nor could we come together into judgment:
text.KJV: 9:32 For he is not a man as I am, that I should answere him, and we should come together in iudgement.
text.NABRE: For he is not a man like myself, that I should answer him, that we should come together in judgment.

text.NRSV: There is no umpire between us, who might lay his hand on us both.
text.Rotherham: There is not, between us, a mediator, who might lay his hand upon us both.
text.KJV: 9:33 Neither is there any dayes-man betwixt vs, that might lay his hand vpon vs both.
text.NABRE: Would that there were an arbiter between us, who could lay his hand upon us both

text.NRSV: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me.
text.Rotherham: Let him take from off me his rod, and, his terror, let it not startle me:
text.KJV: 9:34 Let him take his rodde away from me, & let not his feare terrifie me:
text.NABRE: and withdraw his rod from me, So that his terrors did not frighten me;

text.NRSV: Then I would speak without fear of him, for I know I am not what I am thought to be.
text.Rotherham: I could speak, and not be afraid of him, although, not so, am, I, in myself!
text.KJV: 9:35 Then would I speake, and not feare him; but it is not so with me.
text.NABRE: that I might speak without being afraid of him. Since this is not the case with me, I loathe my life.

text.NRSV: “I loathe my life; I will give free utterance to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
text.Rotherham: My soul doth loathe my life,—I let loose my complaint, I speak, in the bitterness of my soul.
text.KJV: 10:1 My soule is weary of my life, I will leaue my complaint vpon my selfe; I will speake in the bitternesse of my soule.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 I will give myself up to complaint; I will speak from the bitterness of my soul.

text.NRSV: I will say to God, Do not condemn me; let me know why you contend with me.
text.Rotherham: I say unto GOD, Do not hold me guilty, Let me know, on what account thou contendest with me!
text.KJV: 10:2 I will say vnto God, Doe not condemne mee; shewe me wherefore thou contendest with me.
text.NABRE: I will say to God: Do not put me in the wrong! Let me know why you oppose me.

text.NRSV: Does it seem good to you to oppress, to despise the work of your hands and favor the schemes of the wicked?
text.Rotherham: Is it seemly in thee, that thou shouldst oppress? that thou shouldst despise the labour of thine own hand, when, upon the counsel of the lawless, thou hast shone?
text.KJV: 10:3 Is it good vnto thee, that thou shouldest oppresse? that thou shouldest despise the worke of thine hands? and shine vpon the counsell of the wicked?
text.NABRE: Is it a pleasure for you to oppress, to spurn the work of your hands, and shine on the plan of the wicked?

text.NRSV: Do you have eyes of flesh? Do you see as humans see?
text.Rotherham: Eyes of flesh, hast thou? or, as a mortal seeth, seest thou?
text.KJV: 10:4 Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?
text.NABRE: Have you eyes of flesh? Do you see as mortals see?

text.NRSV: Are your days like the days of mortals, or your years like human years,
text.Rotherham: As the days of a mortal, are thy days? or, thy years, as the days of a man?
text.KJV: 10:5 Are thy dayes as the dayes of man? are thy yeeres as mans dayes,
text.NABRE: Are your days like the days of a mortal, and are your years like a human lifetime,

text.NRSV: that you seek out my iniquity and search for my sin,
text.Rotherham: That thou shouldst seek for mine iniquity, and, for my sin, shouldst make search:
text.KJV: 10:6 That thou enquirest after mine iniquitie, and searchest after my sinne?
text.NABRE: That you seek for guilt in me and search after my sins,

text.NRSV: although you know that I am not guilty, and there is no one to deliver out of your hand?
text.Rotherham: Though it is, within thine own knowledge, that I would not be lawless, and, none, out of thy hand, can deliver?
text.KJV: 10:7 Thou knowest that I am not wicked, and there is none that can deliuer out of thine hand.
text.NABRE: Even though you know that I am not wicked, and that none can deliver me out of your hand?

text.NRSV: Your hands fashioned and made me; and now you turn and destroy me.
text.Rotherham: Thine own hands, shaped me, and made me, all in unison round about, and yet thou hast confounded me.
text.KJV: 10:8 Thine hands haue made me and fashioned me together round about yet thou doest destroy me.
text.NABRE: Your hands have formed me and fashioned me; will you then turn and destroy me?

text.NRSV: Remember that you fashioned me like clay; and will you turn me to dust again?
text.Rotherham: Remember, I pray thee, that, as clay, thou didst make me, and, unto dust, thou wilt cause me to return.
text.KJV: 10:9 Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay, and wilt thou bring me into dust againe?
text.NABRE: Oh, remember that you fashioned me from clay! Will you then bring me down to dust again?

text.NRSV: Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
text.Rotherham: Didst thou not, like milk, pour me forth? and, as cheese, curdle me?
text.KJV: 10:10 Hast thou not powred me out as milke, and cruddled me like cheese?
text.NABRE: Did you not pour me out like milk, and thicken me like cheese?

text.NRSV: You clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.
text.Rotherham: With skin and flesh, clothe me? and, with bones and sinews, interweave me?
text.KJV: 10:11 Thou hast cloathed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinewes.
text.NABRE: With skin and flesh you clothed me, with bones and sinews knit me together.

text.NRSV: You have granted me life and steadfast love, and your care has preserved my spirit.
text.Rotherham: Life and lovingkindness, thou didst bestow upon me,—and, thy watchful care, preserved my breath.
text.KJV: 10:12 Thou hast granted me life and fauour, and thy visitation hath preserued my spirit.
text.NABRE: Life and love you granted me, and your providence has preserved my spirit.

text.NRSV: Yet these things you hid in your heart; I know that this was your purpose.
text.Rotherham: Yet, these things, thou didst hide in thy heart, I know that, this, hath been with thee!
text.KJV: 10:13 And these things hast thou hid in thine heart; I know that this is with thee.
text.NABRE: Yet these things you have hidden in your heart; I know they are your purpose:

text.NRSV: If I sin, you watch me, and do not acquit me of my iniquity.
text.Rotherham: If I have sinned, then couldst thou watch me, and, from mine iniquity, thou wouldst not acquit me:
text.KJV: 10:14 If I sinne, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquite me from mine iniquitie.
text.NABRE: If I should sin, you would keep a watch on me, and from my guilt you would not absolve me.

text.NRSV: If I am wicked, woe to me! If I am righteous, I cannot lift up my head, for I am filled with disgrace and look upon my misery.
text.Rotherham: If I have been lawless, alas for me! Or, if I am righteous, I will not lift up my head, Surfeited with shame, look thou then on my humiliation.
text.KJV: 10:15 If I be wicked, woe vnto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift vp my head: I am full of confusion, therefore see thou mine affliction:
text.NABRE: If I should be wicked, alas for me! even if righteous, I dare not hold up my head, sated with shame, drenched in affliction!

text.NRSV: Bold as a lion you hunt me; you repeat your exploits against me.
text.Rotherham: When it is lifted up, like a howling lion, thou dost hunt me, Then again thou dost shew thyself marvellous against me.
text.KJV: 10:16 For it increaseth: thou huntest me as a fierce Lion: and againe thou shewest thy selfe marueilous vpon me.
text.NABRE: Should it lift up, you hunt me like a lion: repeatedly you show your wondrous power against me,

text.NRSV: You renew your witnesses against me, and increase your vexation toward me; you bring fresh troops against me.
text.Rotherham: Thou renewest thy witnesses before me, and dost increase thy vexation with me, Relays—yea an army, is with me.
text.KJV: 10:17 Thou renuest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation vpon me; Changes and warre are against me.
text.NABRE: You renew your attack upon me and multiply your harassment of me; in waves your troops come against me.

text.NRSV: “Why did you bring me forth from the womb? Would that I had died before any eye had seen me,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore then, from the womb, didst thou bring me forth? I might have breathed my last, and, no eye, have seen me.
text.KJV: 10:18 Wherfore then hast thou brought me forth out of the wombe? Oh that I had giuen vp the ghost, and no eye had seene me!
text.NABRE: Why then did you bring me forth from the womb? I should have died and no eye have seen me.

text.NRSV: and were as though I had not been, carried from the womb to the grave.
text.Rotherham: As though I had not been, should I have become,—from the womb to the grave, might I have been borne.
text.KJV: 10:19 I should haue bene as though I had not bene, I should haue bene caried from the wombe to the graue.
text.NABRE: I should be as though I had never lived; I should have been taken from the womb to the grave.

text.NRSV: Are not the days of my life few? Let me alone, that I may find a little comfort
text.Rotherham: Are not my days, few?—then forbear, and set me aside, that I may brighten up for a little;
text.KJV: 10:20 Are not my dayes few? cease then, and let me alone that I may take comfort a litle,
text.NABRE: Are not my days few? Stop! Let me alone, that I may recover a little

text.NRSV: before I go, never to return, to the land of gloom and deep darkness,
text.Rotherham: Before I go, and not return, unto a land of darkness and death-shade:
text.KJV: 10:21 Before I goe whence I shall not returne, euen to the land of darknes and the shadow of death,
text.NABRE: Before I go whence I shall not return, to the land of darkness and of gloom,

text.NRSV: the land of utter gloom and chaos, where light is as darkness.”
text.Rotherham: A land of obscurity, like thick darkness, of death-shade and disorder, and which shineth like thick darkness.
text.KJV: 10:22 A land of darknes, as darknes it selfe, and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkenes.
text.NABRE: The dark, disordered land where darkness is the only light.

text.NRSV: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Zophar the Naamathite, and said:—
text.KJV: 11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 Zophar’s First Speech. And Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Should a multitude of words go unanswered, and should one full of talk be vindicated?
text.Rotherham: Should, the multitude of words, not be answered? Or should, a man full of talk, be justified?
text.KJV: 11:2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should man ful of talke be iustified?
text.NABRE: Should not many words be answered, or must the garrulous man necessarily be right?

text.NRSV: Should your babble put others to silence, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?
text.Rotherham: Shall, thy pratings, cause men to hold their peace? When thou hast mocked, shall there be none to put thee to shame?
text.KJV: 11:3 Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?
text.NABRE: Shall your babblings keep others silent, and shall you deride and no one give rebuke?

text.NRSV: For you say, ‘My conduct is pure, and I am clean in God’s sight.’
text.Rotherham: Since thou hast said, Right is my doctrine, and pure am I in his eyes.
text.KJV: 11:4 For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am cleane in thine eyes.
text.NABRE: Shall you say: “My teaching is pure, and I am clean in your sight”?

text.NRSV: But O that God would speak, and open his lips to you,
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, oh that GOD would speak, that he would open his lips with thee:
text.KJV: 11:5 But, O that God would speake, and open his lippes against thee,
text.NABRE: But oh, that God would speak, and open his lips against you,

text.NRSV: and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For wisdom is many-sided. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
text.Rotherham: That he would declare to thee the secrets of wisdom, for they are double to that which actually is,—Know then that GOD could bring into forgetfulness for thee, a portion of thine iniquity.
text.KJV: 11:6 And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisedome, that they are double to that which is: know therefore that God exacteth of thee lesse then thine iniquitie deserueth.
text.NABRE: And tell you the secrets of wisdom, for good sense has two sides; So you might learn that God overlooks some of your sinfulness.

text.NRSV: “Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?
text.Rotherham: The hidden depth of GOD canst thou discover? Or, unto the furthest limit of the Almighty, canst thou attain?
text.KJV: 11:7 Canst thou by searching finde out God? canst thou finde out the Almightie vnto perfection?
text.NABRE: Can you find out the depths of God? or find out the perfection of the Almighty?

text.NRSV: It is higher than heaven—what can you do? Deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
text.Rotherham: The heights of the heavens, what canst thou do? Depths deeper than hades, what canst thou know?
text.KJV: 11:8 It is as high as heauen, what canst thou doe? deeper then hell, what canst thou know?
text.NABRE: It is higher than the heavens; what can you do? It is deeper than Sheol; what can you know?

text.NRSV: Its measure is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
text.Rotherham: Longer than the earth, is the measure thereof, and broader than the sea.
text.KJV: 11:9 The measure therof is longer then the earth, and broader then the sea.
text.NABRE: It is longer than the earth in measure, and broader than the sea.

text.NRSV: If he passes through, and imprisons, and assembles for judgment, who can hinder him?
text.Rotherham: If he sweep on, or shut up, or call together, Who then shall hinder him?
text.KJV: 11:10 If he cut off, and shut vp, or gather together, then who can hinder him?
text.NABRE: If he should seize and imprison or call to judgment, who then could turn him back?

text.NRSV: For he knows those who are worthless; when he sees iniquity, will he not consider it?
text.Rotherham: For, he, knoweth men of falsity, and seeth iniquity, and him that doth not diligently consider.
text.KJV: 11:11 For, he knoweth vaine men: hee seeth wickednesse also, will he not then consider it?
text.NABRE: For he knows the worthless and sees iniquity; will he then ignore it?

text.NRSV: But a stupid person will get understanding, when a wild donkey is born human.
text.Rotherham: But, an empty person, will get sense, when, a wild ass’s colt, is born a man!
text.KJV: 11:12 For vaine man would be wise; though man be borne like a wilde asses coult.
text.NABRE: An empty head will gain understanding, when a colt of a wild jackass is born human.

text.NRSV: “If you direct your heart rightly, you will stretch out your hands toward him.
text.Rotherham: If, thou, hast prepared thy heart, and wilt spread forth, unto him, thy hands—
text.KJV: 11:13 If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him:
text.NABRE: If you set your heart aright and stretch out your hands toward him,

text.NRSV: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away, and do not let wickedness reside in your tents.
text.Rotherham: If, iniquity, be in thy hand, Put it far away, and let there not dwell in thy tents perversity,
text.KJV: 11:14 If iniquitie be in thine hand, put it farre away, and let not wickednes dwell in thy tabernacles.
text.NABRE: If iniquity is in your hand, remove it, and do not let injustice dwell in your tent,

text.NRSV: Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure, and will not fear.
text.Rotherham: Surely, then, shalt thou lift up thy face free from blemish, and shalt be established, and not fear.
text.KJV: 11:15 For then shalt thou lift vp thy face without spot, yea thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not feare:
text.NABRE: Surely then you may lift up your face in innocence; you may stand firm and unafraid.

text.NRSV: You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.
text.Rotherham: For, now, shalt thou forget, sorrow, Like waters passed away, shalt thou remember it.
text.KJV: 11:16 Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that passe away:
text.NABRE: For then you shall forget your misery, like water that has ebbed away you shall regard it.

text.NRSV: And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
text.Rotherham: Above high noon, shall rise life’s continuance, Darkness, like a morning, shall appear,
text.KJV: 11:17 And thine age shalbe clearer then the noone day; thou shalt shine foorth, thou shalt be as the morning.
text.NABRE: Then your life shall be brighter than the noonday; its gloom shall become like the morning,

text.NRSV: And you will have confidence, because there is hope; you will be protected and take your rest in safety.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt he confident, that there is hope, and, when thou hast searched, securely shalt thou lie down;
text.KJV: 11:18 And thou shalt be secure because there is hope, yea thou shalt digge about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
text.NABRE: And you shall be secure, because there is hope; you shall look round you and lie down in safety;

text.NRSV: You will lie down, and no one will make you afraid; many will entreat your favor.
text.Rotherham: And shalt rest, with none to put thee in terror,—and many shall entreat thy favour.
text.KJV: 11:19 Also thou shalt lye downe, and none shall make thee afraid; yea many shall make suite vnto thee.
text.NABRE: you shall lie down and no one will disturb you. Many shall entreat your favor,

text.NRSV: But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them, and their hope is to breathe their last.”
text.Rotherham: But, the eyes of the lawless, shall fail,—and, place of refuge, shall have vanished from them, and, their hope, be a breathing out of life.
text.KJV: 11:20 But the eyes of the wicked shall faile, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giuing vp of the ghost.
text.NABRE: but the wicked, looking on, shall be consumed with envy. Escape shall be cut off from them, their only hope their last breath.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 12:1 And Iob answered, and sayd,
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Job’s Third Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “No doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.
text.Rotherham: Of a truth, ye, are the people, and, with you, wisdom, will die.
text.KJV: 12:2 No doubt but ye are the people, and wisedome shall die with you.
text.NABRE: No doubt you are the people with whom wisdom shall die!

text.NRSV: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
text.Rotherham: I also, have a mind like you, I, fall not short, of you, But who hath not such things as these?
text.KJV: 12:3 But I haue vnderstanding as well as you, I am not inferiour to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?
text.NABRE: But I have intelligence as well as you; I do not fall short of you; for who does not know such things as these?

text.NRSV: I am a laughingstock to my friends; I, who called upon God and he answered me, a just and blameless man, I am a laughingstock.
text.Rotherham: A laughing-stock to one’s neighbour, do I become, one who hath called upon GOD and he hath answered him! A laughing-stock—a righteous man without blame!
text.KJV: 12:4 I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth vpon God, and he answereth him: the iust vpright man is laughed to scorne.
text.NABRE: I have become the sport of my neighbors: “The one whom God answers when he calls upon him, The just, the perfect man,” is a laughingstock;

text.NRSV: Those at ease have contempt for misfortune, but it is ready for those whose feet are unstable.
text.Rotherham: For ruin, there is contempt, in the thought of the man at ease,—ready, for such as are of faltering foot!
text.KJV: 12:5 He that is ready to slippe with his feet, is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
text.NABRE: The undisturbed esteem my downfall a disgrace such as awaits unsteady feet;

text.NRSV: The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure, who bring their god in their hands.
text.Rotherham: At peace are the tents that belong to the spoilers, and there is security to them who provoke GOD, To him who bringeth a god in his hand.
text.KJV: 12:6 The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that prouoke God are secure, into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.
text.NABRE: Yet the tents of robbers are prosperous, and those who provoke God are secure, whom God has in his power.

text.NRSV: “But ask the animals, and they will teach you; the birds of the air, and they will tell you;
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, ask, I pray thee, the beasts, and they will teach thee, and the bird of the heavens, and it will tell thee;
text.KJV: 12:7 But aske now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the foules of the aire, and they shall tell thee.
text.NABRE: But now ask the beasts to teach you, the birds of the air to tell you;

text.NRSV: or ask the plants of the earth, and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
text.Rotherham: Or address the earth, and it will teach thee, and the fishes of the sea, will recount it to thee:
text.KJV: 12:8 Or speake to the earth, and it shall teach thee; and the fishes of the sea shall declare vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Or speak to the earth to instruct you, and the fish of the sea to inform you.

text.NRSV: Who among all these does not know that the hand of the Lord has done this?
text.Rotherham: Who knoweth not, among all these, that, the hand of Yahweh, hath done this?
text.KJV: 12:9 Who knoweth not in all these, that the hand of the Lord hath wrought this?
text.NABRE: Which of all these does not know that the hand of God has done this?

text.NRSV: In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of every human being.
text.Rotherham: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the spirit of all the flesh of men.
text.KJV: 12:10 In whose hand is the soule of euery liuing thing, and the breath of all mankinde.
text.NABRE: In his hand is the soul of every living thing, and the life breath of all mortal flesh.

text.NRSV: Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
text.Rotherham: Doth not, the ear, try, words? even as, the palate, tasteth for itself, food?
text.KJV: 12:11 Doeth not the eare trie wordes? and the mouth taste his meate?
text.NABRE: Does not the ear judge words as the mouth tastes food?

text.NRSV: Is wisdom with the aged, and understanding in length of days?
text.Rotherham: In the Ancient, is wisdom, and [in] Length of Days, understanding:
text.KJV: 12:12 With the ancient is wisedome, and in length of dayes, vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: So with old age is wisdom, and with length of days understanding.

text.NRSV: “With God are wisdom and might; he has counsel and understanding.
text.Rotherham: With Him, are wisdom and strength, to Him, pertain counsel and understanding.
text.KJV: 12:13 With him is wisedome & strength, he hath counsell and vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: With him are wisdom and might; his are counsel and understanding.

text.NRSV: If he tears down, no one can rebuild; if he imprisons, no one can set free.
text.Rotherham: Lo! He pulleth down, and it cannot be built, He closeth up over a man, and it cannot be opened:
text.KJV: 12:14 Behold, he breaketh downe, and it cannot be built againe: hee shutteth vp a man, and there can be no opening.
text.NABRE: If he knocks a thing down, there is no rebuilding; if he imprisons, there is no release.

text.NRSV: If he withholds the waters, they dry up; if he sends them out, they overwhelm the land.
text.Rotherham: Lo! He holdeth back the waters, and they dry up, or sendeth them out, and they transform the earth:
text.KJV: 12:15 Behold, hee withholdeth the waters, and they drie vp: also hee sendeth them out, and they ouerturne the earth.
text.NABRE: He holds back the waters and there is drought; he sends them forth and they overwhelm the land.

text.NRSV: With him are strength and wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
text.Rotherham: With Him, is strength and effective wisdom, to Him, belong he that erreth, and he that causeth to err.
text.KJV: 12:16 With him is strength & wisedome: the deceiued, and the deceiuer, are his.
text.NABRE: With him are strength and prudence; the misled and the misleaders are his.

text.NRSV: He leads counselors away stripped, and makes fools of judges.
text.Rotherham: Who leadeth away counsellors [as] a spoil, and, judges, He befooleth:
text.KJV: 12:17 He leadeth counsellers away spoiled, and maketh the Iudges fooles.
text.NABRE: He sends counselors away barefoot, makes fools of judges.

text.NRSV: He looses the sash of kings, and binds a waistcloth on their loins.
text.Rotherham: The fetters of kings, He looseth, or hath bound a slave’s waistcloth about their loins:
text.KJV: 12:18 He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loines with a girdle.
text.NABRE: He loosens the belt of kings, ties a waistcloth on their loins.

text.NRSV: He leads priests away stripped, and overthrows the mighty.
text.Rotherham: Who leadeth away priests [as] a spoil, and, men firmly seated, He overturneth:
text.KJV: 12:19 He leadeth Princes away spoiled, and ouerthroweth the mightie.
text.NABRE: He sends priests away barefoot, leads the powerful astray.

text.NRSV: He deprives of speech those who are trusted, and takes away the discernment of the elders.
text.Rotherham: Setting aside the speech of the trusty, and, the discernment of elders, He taketh away:
text.KJV: 12:20 He remooueth away the speech of the trustie, and taketh away the vnderstanding of the aged.
text.NABRE: He silences the trusted adviser, takes discretion from the elders.

text.NRSV: He pours contempt on princes, and looses the belt of the strong.
text.Rotherham: Pouring contempt upon nobles, and, the girdle of the mighty, hath He loosed:
text.KJV: 12:21 He powreth contempt vpon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mightie.
text.NABRE: He pours shame on nobles, the waistband of the strong he loosens.

text.NRSV: He uncovers the deeps out of darkness, and brings deep darkness to light.
text.Rotherham: Laying open deep things, out of darkness, and bringing out to light, the death-shade:
text.KJV: 12:22 Hee discouereth deepe things out of darkenesse, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
text.NABRE: He uncovers deep things from the darkness, brings the gloom into the light.

text.NRSV: He makes nations great, then destroys them; he enlarges nations, then leads them away.
text.Rotherham: Who giveth greatness to nations, or destroyeth them, Who spreadeth out nations, or leadeth them into exile:
text.KJV: 12:23 He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: hee inlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them againe.
text.NABRE: He makes nations great and destroys them, spreads peoples abroad and abandons them.

text.NRSV: He strips understanding from the leaders of the earth, and makes them wander in a pathless waste.
text.Rotherham: Who taketh away the sense of the chiefs of the people of the earth, and hath caused them to wander in a pathless waste:
text.KJV: 12:24 He taketh away the heart of the chiefe of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wildernes where there is no way.
text.NABRE: He takes understanding from the leaders of the land, makes them wander in a pathless desert.

text.NRSV: They grope in the dark without light; he makes them stagger like a drunkard.
text.Rotherham: They grope about in the dark, having no light, and He hath made them to reel, like a drunken man.
text.KJV: 12:25 They grope in the darke without light, and hee maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.
text.NABRE: They grope in the darkness without light; he makes them wander like drunkards.

text.NRSV: “Look, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it.
text.Rotherham: Lo! all [this], hath mine own eye seen,—Mine ear hath heard and understood it:
text.KJV: 13:1 Loe, mine eye hath seene all this, mine eare hath heard and vnderstood it.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 All this my eye has seen; my ear has heard and perceived it.

text.NRSV: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
text.Rotherham: Just as ye know, I too, know, I, fall not short, of you.
text.KJV: 13:2 What yee know, the same doe I know also, I am not inferiour vnto you.
text.NABRE: What you know, I also know; I do not fall short of you.

text.NRSV: But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
text.Rotherham: But indeed, I, unto the Almighty, would speak, and, to direct my argument unto GOD, would I be well pleased.
text.KJV: 13:3 Surely I would speake to the Almighty, & I desire to reason with God.
text.NABRE: But I would speak with the Almighty; I want to argue with God.

text.NRSV: As for you, you whitewash with lies; all of you are worthless physicians.
text.Rotherham: For, in truth, ye, do besmear with falsehood, Worthless physicians, all of you!
text.KJV: 13:4 But ye are forgers of lies, yee are all Physicians of no value.
text.NABRE: But you gloss over falsehoods, you are worthless physicians, every one of you!

text.NRSV: If you would only keep silent, that would be your wisdom!
text.Rotherham: Oh that ye would, altogether hold your peace, and it should serve you for wisdom!
text.KJV: 13:5 O that you would altogether hold your peace, & it should be your wisdome.
text.NABRE: Oh, that you would be altogether silent; that for you would be wisdom!

text.NRSV: Hear now my reasoning, and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
text.Rotherham: Hear, I pray you, the argument of my mouth, and, to the pleadings of my lips, give heed:—
text.KJV: 13:6 Heare now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
text.NABRE: Hear now my argument and listen to the accusations from my lips.

text.NRSV: Will you speak falsely for God, and speak deceitfully for him?
text.Rotherham: Is it, for God, ye would speak perversely? And, for him, would ye speak deceit:
text.KJV: 13:7 Wil you speake wickedly for God? and talke deceitfully for him?
text.NABRE: Is it for God that you speak falsehood? Is it for him that you utter deceit?

text.NRSV: Will you show partiality toward him, will you plead the case for God?
text.Rotherham: Even, for him, would ye be partial? Or, for GOD, would ye [so] plead?
text.KJV: 13:8 Will ye accept his person? Will yee contend for God?
text.NABRE: Is it for him that you show partiality? Do you make accusations on behalf of God?

text.NRSV: Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him, as one person deceives another?
text.Rotherham: Would it be well, when he searched you out? Or, as one might jest with a mortal, would ye jest, with him?
text.KJV: 13:9 Is it good that hee should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, doe ye so mocke him?
text.NABRE: Will it be well when he shall search you out? Can you deceive him as you do a mere human being?

text.NRSV: He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
text.Rotherham: He will, severely rebuke, you, if ye are secretly partial.
text.KJV: 13:10 He will surely reprooue you, if yee doe secretly accept persons.
text.NABRE: He will openly rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.

text.NRSV: Will not his majesty terrify you, and the dread of him fall upon you?
text.Rotherham: Shall not, his majesty, overwhelm you? and, the dread of him, fall upon you?
text.KJV: 13:11 Shall not his excellencie make you afraid? and his dread fall vpon you?
text.NABRE: Surely his majesty will frighten you and dread of him fall upon you.

text.NRSV: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes, your defenses are defenses of clay.
text.Rotherham: Are not your memorable sayings, proverbs of ashes? Breastworks of clay, your breastworks?
text.KJV: 13:12 Your remembrances are like vnto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.
text.NABRE: Your reminders are ashy maxims, your fabrications mounds of clay.

text.NRSV: “Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.
text.Rotherham: Quietly let me alone, that, I, may speak out, then let come on me, what may.
text.KJV: 13:13 Hold your peace, let me alone that I may speake, and let come on me what will.
text.NABRE: Be silent! Let me alone that I may speak, no matter what happens to me.

text.NRSV: I will take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in my hand.
text.Rotherham: In any case, I will take up my flesh in my teeth, and, my life, will I put in my hand:
text.KJV: 13:14 Wherefore doe I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?
text.NABRE: I will carry my flesh between my teeth, and take my life in my hand.

text.NRSV: See, he will kill me; I have no hope; but I will defend my ways to his face.
text.Rotherham: Lo! he may slay me, [yet], for him, will I wait,—Nevertheless, my ways—unto his face, will I show to be right:
text.KJV: 13:15 Though hee slay mee, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintaine mine owne wayes before him.
text.NABRE: Slay me though he might, I will wait for him; I will defend my conduct before him.

text.NRSV: This will be my salvation, that the godless shall not come before him.
text.Rotherham: Even he, will be on my side—unto salvation, For, not before his face, shall any impious person come.
text.KJV: 13:16 Hee also shall be my saluation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.
text.NABRE: This shall be my salvation: no impious man can come into his presence.

text.NRSV: Listen carefully to my words, and let my declaration be in your ears.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye patiently my speech, and be my declaration in your ears.
text.KJV: 13:17 Heare diligently my speach, and my declaration with your eares.
text.NABRE: Pay close attention to my speech, give my statement a hearing.

text.NRSV: I have indeed prepared my case; I know that I shall be vindicated.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I pray you, I have set forth in order a plea, I know that, I, shall be found right.
text.KJV: 13:18 Behold now, I haue ordered my cause, I know that I shall be iustified.
text.NABRE: Behold, I have prepared my case, I know that I am in the right.

text.NRSV: Who is there that will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
text.Rotherham: Who is it that shall contend with me? For, now, if I should hold my peace, why! I should breathe my last!
text.KJV: 13:19 Who is hee that will plead with me? for now if I hold my tongue, I shall giue vp the ghost.
text.NABRE: If anyone can make a case against me, then I shall be silent and expire.

text.NRSV: Only grant two things to me, then I will not hide myself from your face:
text.Rotherham: Only, two things, do thou not with me, then, from thy face, will I not hide me:—
text.KJV: 13:20 Only doe not two things vnto me: then will I not hide my selfe from thee.
text.NABRE: Two things only do not use against me, then from your presence I need not hide:

text.NRSV: withdraw your hand far from me, and do not let dread of you terrify me.
text.Rotherham: Thy hand—from off me, take thou far away, and, thy terror, let it not startle me!
text.KJV: 13:21 Withdrawe thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make mee afraid.
text.NABRE: Withdraw your hand far from me, do not let the terror of you frighten me.

text.NRSV: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.
text.Rotherham: Then call thou, and, I, will answer, Or I will speak, and reply thou unto me.
text.KJV: 13:22 Then call thou, and I will answere: or let me speake, and answere thou mee.
text.NABRE: Then call me, and I will respond; or let me speak first, and answer me.

text.NRSV: How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
text.Rotherham: How many are mine iniquities and sins? My transgression and my sin, let me know!
text.KJV: 13:23 How many are mine iniquities and sinnes? make mee to knowe my transgression, and my sinne.
text.NABRE: What are my faults and my sins? My misdeed, my sin make known to me!

text.NRSV: Why do you hide your face, and count me as your enemy?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, thy face, shouldst thou hide? Or count me, as an enemy to thee?
text.KJV: 13:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemie?
text.NABRE: Why do you hide your face and consider me your enemy?

text.NRSV: Will you frighten a settled leaf and pursue dry chaff?
text.Rotherham: A driven leaf, wilt thou cause to tremble? Or, dry stubble, wilt thou pursue?
text.KJV: 13:25 Wilt thou breake a leafe driuen to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the drie stubble?
text.NABRE: Will you harass a wind-driven leaf or pursue a withered straw?

text.NRSV: For you write bitter things against me, and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
text.Rotherham: For thou writest, against me, bitter things, and dost make me inherit the iniquities of my youth;
text.KJV: 13:26 For thou writest bitter things against mee, and makest me to possesse the iniquities of my youth.
text.NABRE: For you draw up bitter indictments against me, and punish in me the faults of my youth.

text.NRSV: You put my feet in the stocks, and watch all my paths; you set a bound to the soles of my feet.
text.Rotherham: And thou dost put—in the stocks—my feet, and observest all my paths, Against the roots of my feet, thou dost cut out a bound;
text.KJV: 13:27 Thou puttest my feete also in the stockes, and lookest narrowly vnto all my pathes; thou settest a print vpon the heeles of my feete.
text.NABRE: You put my feet in the stocks; you watch all my paths and trace out all my footsteps,

text.NRSV: One wastes away like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.”
text.Rotherham: And, a man himself, as a rotten thing, weareth out, as a garment which the moth hath eaten.
text.KJV: 13:28 And hee, as a rotten thing consumeth, as a garment that is moth-eaten.
text.NABRE: Though I wear out like a leather bottle, like a garment the moth has consumed.

text.NRSV: “A mortal, born of woman, few of days and full of trouble,
text.Rotherham: Man that is born of a woman, is of few days, and full of trouble:
text.KJV: 14:1 Man that is borne of a woman, is of few dayes, and full of trouble.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Man born of woman is short-lived and full of trouble,

text.NRSV: comes up like a flower and withers, flees like a shadow and does not last.
text.Rotherham: As a flower, he cometh forth—and fadeth, He fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.
text.KJV: 14:2 Hee commeth forth like a flower, and is cut downe: he fleeth also, as a shaddow and continueth not.
text.NABRE: Like a flower that springs up and fades, swift as a shadow that does not abide.

text.NRSV: Do you fix your eyes on such a one? Do you bring me into judgment with you?
text.Rotherham: And yet upon such a one as this, hast thou opened thine eye? And, him, wouldst thou bring into judgment with thee?
text.KJV: 14:3 And doest thou open thine eies vpon such an one, and bringest me into iudgment with thee?
text.NABRE: Upon such a one will you set your eyes, bringing me into judgment before you?

text.NRSV: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one can.
text.Rotherham: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? Not one!
text.KJV: 14:4 Who can bring a cleane thing out of an vncleane? not one.
text.NABRE: Can anyone make the unclean clean? No one can.

text.NRSV: Since their days are determined, and the number of their months is known to you, and you have appointed the bounds that they cannot pass,
text.Rotherham: If determined am his days, the number of his months, is with thee, Fixed times for him, thou hast appointed and he cannot go beyond.
text.KJV: 14:5 Seeing his daies are determined, the number of his moneths are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot passe.
text.NABRE: Since his days are determined— you know the number of his months; you have fixed the limit which he cannot pass—

text.NRSV: look away from them, and desist, that they may enjoy, like laborers, their days.
text.Rotherham: Look sway from him, that he may rest, Till he shall pay off, as a hireling, his day.
text.KJV: 14:6 Turne from him that hee may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hircling, his day.
text.NABRE: Look away from him and let him be, while, like a hireling, he completes his day.

text.NRSV: “For there is hope for a tree, if it is cut down, that it will sprout again, and that its shoots will not cease.
text.Rotherham: Though there is—for a tree—hope,—if it should be cut down, that, again, it will grow, and, the tender branch thereof, will not cease;
text.KJV: 14:7 For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut downe, that it will sprout againe, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.
text.NABRE: For a tree there is hope; if it is cut down, it will sprout again, its tender shoots will not cease.

text.NRSV: Though its root grows old in the earth, and its stump dies in the ground,
text.Rotherham: If its root, should become old in the earth, and, in the dust, its stock should die:
text.KJV: 14:8 Though the roote thereof waxe old in the earth, and the stocke thereof die in the ground:
text.NABRE: Even though its root grow old in the earth and its stump die in the dust,

text.NRSV: yet at the scent of water it will bud and put forth branches like a young plant.
text.Rotherham: Through the scent of water, it may break forth, and produce branches like a sapling,
text.KJV: 14:9 Yet through the sent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughes like a plant.
text.NABRE: Yet at the first whiff of water it sprouts and puts forth branches like a young plant.

text.NRSV: But mortals die, and are laid low; humans expire, and where are they?
text.Rotherham: Yet, man, dieth, and is prostrate, Yea the son of earth doth cease to breathe, and where is he?
text.KJV: 14:10 But man dyeth, and wasteth away; yea, man giueth vp the ghost, and where is hee?
text.NABRE: But when a man dies, all vigor leaves him; when a mortal expires, where then is he?

text.NRSV: As waters fail from a lake, and a river wastes away and dries up,
text.Rotherham: Waters, have failed from, the sea, and, a river, may waste and dry up;
text.KJV: 14:11 As the waters faile from the sea, and the floud decayeth and dryeth vp:
text.NABRE: As when the waters of a lake fail, or a stream shrivels and dries up,

text.NRSV: so mortals lie down and do not rise again; until the heavens are no more, they will not awake or be roused out of their sleep.
text.Rotherham: So, a man, hath lain down, and shall not arise, until there are no heavens, they shall not awake, nor be roused up out of their sleep.
text.KJV: 14:12 So man lyeth downe, and riseth not, till the heauens be no more, they shall not awake; nor bee raised out of their sleepe.
text.NABRE: So mortals lie down, never to rise. Until the heavens are no more, they shall not awake, nor be roused out of their sleep.

text.NRSV: O that you would hide me in Sheol, that you would conceal me until your wrath is past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!
text.Rotherham: Oh that, in hades, thou wouldst hide me! that thou wouldst keep me secret, until the turn of thine anger, that thou wouldst set for me a fixed time, and remember me:
text.KJV: 14:13 O that thou wouldest hide mee in the graue, that thou wouldest keepe me secret, vntill thy wrath bee past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me.
text.NABRE: Oh, that you would hide me in Sheol, shelter me till your wrath is past, fix a time to remember me!

text.NRSV: If mortals die, will they live again? All the days of my service I would wait until my release should come.
text.Rotherham: If a man die, can he live again? All the days of my warfare, would I wait, until my relief should come:—
text.KJV: 14:14 If a man die, shall he liue againe? All the dayes of my appointed time will I waite, till my change come.
text.NABRE: If a man were to die, and live again, all the days of my drudgery I would wait for my relief to come.

text.NRSV: You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.
text.Rotherham: Thou shouldst call, and, I, would answer thee,—For the work of thine own hand, thou shouldst long.
text.KJV: 14:15 Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt haue a desire to the worke of thine hands.
text.NABRE: You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.

text.NRSV: For then you would not number my steps; you would not keep watch over my sin;
text.Rotherham: For, now, my steps, thou countest, Thou wilt not pass over my sin:
text.KJV: 14:16 For nowe thou numbrest my steppes, doest thou not watch ouer my sinne?
text.NABRE: Surely then you would count my steps, and not keep watch for sin in me.

text.NRSV: my transgression would be sealed up in a bag, and you would cover over my iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Sealed up in a bag, is my transgression, and thou hast glued over mine iniquity.
text.KJV: 14:17 My transgression is sealed vp in a bagge, and thou sowest vp mine iniquitie.
text.NABRE: My misdeeds would be sealed up in a pouch, and you would cover over my guilt.

text.NRSV: “But the mountain falls and crumbles away, and the rock is removed from its place;
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, a mountain falling, will lie prostrate, or, a rock moved out of its place:
text.KJV: 14:18 And surely the mountaine falling commeth to nought: and the rocke is remoued out of his place.
text.NABRE: Mountains fall and crumble, rocks move from their place,

text.NRSV: the waters wear away the stones; the torrents wash away the soil of the earth; so you destroy the hope of mortals.
text.Rotherham: Stones, have been hollowed out by waters, the floods thereof wash away the dust of the earth, and, the hope of mortal man, thou hast destroyed:
text.KJV: 14:19 The waters weare the stones, thou washest away the things which growe out of the dust of the earth, and thou destroyest the hope of man.
text.NABRE: And water wears away stone, and floods wash away the soil of the land— so you destroy the hope of mortals!

text.NRSV: You prevail forever against them, and they pass away; you change their countenance, and send them away.
text.Rotherham: Thou dost overpower him utterly, and he departeth, Disfiguring his face, so, hast thou sent him away.
text.KJV: 14:20 Thou preuailest for euer against him, and hee passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.
text.NABRE: You prevail once for all against them and they pass on; you dismiss them with changed appearance.

text.NRSV: Their children come to honor, and they do not know it; they are brought low, and it is not perceived.
text.Rotherham: His sons, come to honour, and he knoweth it not, Or they are brought low, and he perceiveth it not of them.
text.KJV: 14:21 His sonnes come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought lowe, but he perceiueth it not of them.
text.NABRE: If their children are honored, they are not aware of it; or if disgraced, they do not know about them.

text.NRSV: They feel only the pain of their own bodies, and mourn only for themselves.”
text.Rotherham: But, his flesh, for himself, is in pain, and, his soul, for himself, doth mourn.
text.KJV: 14:22 But his flesh vpon him shall haue paine, and his soule within him shall mourne.
text.NABRE: Only for themselves, their pain; only for themselves, their mourning.

text.NRSV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Eliphaz the Temanite, and said:
text.KJV: 15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,
text.NABRE: III. Second Cycle of Speeches Chapter 15 Second Speech of Eliphaz. Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Should the wise answer with windy knowledge, and fill themselves with the east wind?
text.Rotherham: Should, a wise man, answer unreal knowledge? or fill, with the east wind, his inner man?
text.KJV: 15:2 Should a wise man vtter vaine knowledge, and fill his belly with the East winde?
text.NABRE: Does a wise man answer with windy opinions, or puff himself up with the east wind?

text.NRSV: Should they argue in words that do not profit, or in speeches that do no good?
text.Rotherham: Disputing with discourse that doth no good, or with speech, wherein is no profit?
text.KJV: 15:3 Should hee reason with vnprofitable talke? or with speeches wherewith he can doe no good?
text.NABRE: Does he argue in speech that does not avail, and in words that are to no profit?

text.NRSV: But you are doing away with the fear of God, and hindering meditation before God.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, wouldst take away reverence, and wouldst attain unto meditation before GOD.
text.KJV: 15:4 Yea thou castest off feare, and restrainest prayer before God.
text.NABRE: You in fact do away with piety, you lessen devotion toward God,

text.NRSV: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.
text.Rotherham: For thine own mouth would teach thine iniquity, and thou wouldst choose the tongue of the crafty.
text.KJV: 15:5 For thy mouth vttereth thine iniquitie, and thou choosest the tongue of the craftie.
text.NABRE: Because your wickedness instructs your mouth, and you choose to speak like the crafty.

text.NRSV: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; your own lips testify against you.
text.Rotherham: Thine own mouth shall condemn thee, and, not I, And, thine own lips, shall testify against thee.
text.KJV: 15:6 Thine owne mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea thine owne lippes testifie against thee.
text.NABRE: Your own mouth condemns you, not I; your own lips refute you.

text.NRSV: “Are you the firstborn of the human race? Were you brought forth before the hills?
text.Rotherham: The first of mankind, wast thou born? Or, before the hills, wast thou brought forth?
text.KJV: 15:7 Art thou the first man that was borne? or wast thou made before the hilles?
text.NABRE: Were you the first to be born? Were you brought forth before the hills?

text.NRSV: Have you listened in the council of God? And do you limit wisdom to yourself?
text.Rotherham: In the secret council of GOD, hast thou been wont to hearken? Or canst thou attain for thyself unto wisdom?
text.KJV: 15:8 Hast thou heard the secret of God? and doest thou restraine wisedome to thy selfe?
text.NABRE: Do you listen in on God’s council and restrict wisdom to yourself?

text.NRSV: What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not clear to us?
text.Rotherham: What knowest thou, that we know not? [What] understandest thou, and the same, is not with us?
text.KJV: 15:9 What knowest thou that we know not? what vnderstandest thou, which is not in vs?
text.NABRE: What do you know that we do not know, or understand that we do not?

text.NRSV: The gray-haired and the aged are on our side, those older than your father.
text.Rotherham: Both hoary and venerable, are among us, one mightier than thy father in days!
text.KJV: 15:10 With vs are both the gray headed, and very aged men, much elder then thy father.
text.NABRE: There are gray-haired old men among us, more advanced in years than your father.

text.NRSV: Are the consolations of God too small for you, or the word that deals gently with you?
text.Rotherham: Too small for thee, are the consolations of GOD? or a word spoken gently with thee?
text.KJV: 15:11 Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?
text.NABRE: Are the consolations of God not enough for you, and speech that deals gently with you?

text.NRSV: Why does your heart carry you away, and why do your eyes flash,
text.Rotherham: How doth thine own heart carry thee away, and how thine eyes do roll!
text.KJV: 15:12 Why doeth thine heart carie thee away? and what doe thine eyes winke at,
text.NABRE: Why does your heart carry you away, and why do your eyes flash,

text.NRSV: so that you turn your spirit against God, and let such words go out of your mouth?
text.Rotherham: For thy spirit, replieth against GOD, and thou bringest forth—out of thy mouth—words!
text.KJV: 15:13 That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words goe out of thy mouth?
text.NABRE: So that you turn your anger against God and let such words escape your mouth!

text.NRSV: What are mortals, that they can be clean? Or those born of woman, that they can be righteous?
text.Rotherham: What is a mortal, that he should be pure? or that righteous should be one born of a woman?
text.KJV: 15:14 What is man, that he should be cleane? and he which is borne of a woman, that he should be righteous?
text.NABRE: How can any mortal be blameless, anyone born of woman be righteous?

text.NRSV: God puts no trust even in his holy ones, and the heavens are not clean in his sight;
text.Rotherham: Lo! in his holy ones, he putteth not confidence, and, the heavens, are not pure in his eyes:
text.KJV: 15:15 Beholde, he putteth no trust in his Saints, yea, the heauens are not cleane in his sight.
text.NABRE: If in his holy ones God places no confidence, and if the heavens are not without blame in his sight,

text.NRSV: how much less one who is abominable and corrupt, one who drinks iniquity like water!
text.Rotherham: How much less when one is detested and corrupt, a man who drinketh in—like water—perversity.
text.KJV: 15:16 How much more abominable and filthie is man, which drinketh iniquitie like water?
text.NABRE: How much less so is the abominable and corrupt: people who drink in iniquity like water!

text.NRSV: “I will show you; listen to me; what I have seen I will declare—
text.Rotherham: I will tell thee—hear me, Since this I have seen, I must needs declare it.
text.KJV: 15:17 I will shew thee, heare me, and that which I haue seene, I wil declare,
text.NABRE: I will show you, if you listen to me; what I have seen I will tell—

text.NRSV: what sages have told, and their ancestors have not hidden,
text.Rotherham: Which, wise men, tell, and deny not [that which is] from their fathers.
text.KJV: 15:18 Which wise men haue tolde from their fathers, and haue not hid it:
text.NABRE: What the wise relate and have not contradicted since the days of their ancestors,

text.NRSV: to whom alone the land was given, and no stranger passed among them.
text.Rotherham: To them alone, was the earth given, and no alien passed through their midst:
text.KJV: 15:19 Unto whom alone the earth was giuen, and no stranger passed among them.
text.NABRE: To whom alone the land was given, when no foreigner moved among them:

text.NRSV: The wicked writhe in pain all their days, through all the years that are laid up for the ruthless.
text.Rotherham: All the days of the lawless man, he, doth writhe with pain, and, the number of years, is hidden from the tyrant;
text.KJV: 15:20 The wicked man trauaileth with paine all his dayes, and the number of yeeres is hidden to the oppressour.
text.NABRE: The wicked is in torment all his days, and limited years are in store for the ruthless;

text.NRSV: Terrifying sounds are in their ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon them.
text.Rotherham: A noise of dreadful things, is in his ears, In prosperity, the destroyer cometh upon him;
text.KJV: 15:21 A dreadfull sound is in his eares; in prosperitie the destroyer shall come vpon him.
text.NABRE: The sound of terrors is in his ears; when all is prosperous, a spoiler comes upon him.

text.NRSV: They do not believe that they will return out of darkness, and they are destined for the sword.
text.Rotherham: He hath no confidence to come back out of darkness, he, being destined to the power oft the sword;
text.KJV: 15:22 He beleeueth not that he shall returne out of darkenesse, and he is waited for, of the sword.
text.NABRE: He despairs of escaping the darkness, and looks ever for the sword;

text.NRSV: They wander abroad for bread, saying, ‘Where is it?’ They know that a day of darkness is ready at hand;
text.Rotherham: A wanderer, he, for bread, [saying] Where [is it] ? He knoweth that, prepared by his own hand, is the day of darkness;
text.KJV: 15:23 He wandereth abroad for bread, saying, Where is it? he knoweth that the day of darkenes is ready at his hand.
text.NABRE: A wanderer, food for vultures, he knows destruction is imminent.

text.NRSV: distress and anguish terrify them; they prevail against them, like a king prepared for battle.
text.Rotherham: Distress and anguish shall startle him, It shall overpower him, like a king ready for the onset:
text.KJV: 15:24 Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall preuaile against him, as a king ready to the battell.
text.NABRE: A day of darkness fills him with dread; distress and anguish overpower him, like a king expecting an attack.

text.NRSV: Because they stretched out their hands against God, and bid defiance to the Almighty,
text.Rotherham: Because he had stretched out—against GOD—his hand, and, against the Almighty, had been wont to behave himself proudly;
text.KJV: 15:25 For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himselfe against the Almightie.
text.NABRE: Because he has stretched out his hand against God and arrogantly challenged the Almighty,

text.NRSV: running stubbornly against him with a thick-bossed shield;
text.Rotherham: He used to run against him with uplifted neck, with the stout bosses of his bucklers;
text.KJV: 15:26 He runneth vpon him, euen on his necke, vpon the thicke bosses of his bucklers:
text.NABRE: Rushing defiantly against him, with the stout bosses of his shields.

text.NRSV: because they have covered their faces with their fat, and gathered fat upon their loins,
text.Rotherham: For he had covered his face with his fatness, and had gathered a superabundance on his loins;
text.KJV: 15:27 Because he couereth his face with his fatnesse, and maketh collops of fat on his flankes.
text.NABRE: Although he has covered his face with his crassness, padded his loins with blubber,

text.NRSV: they will live in desolate cities, in houses that no one should inhabit, which are destined to become heaps of ruins;
text.Rotherham: And had inhabited demolished cities, houses, wherein men would not dwell, that were destined to become heaps.
text.KJV: 15:28 And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heapes.
text.NABRE: He shall dwell in ruined cities, in houses that are deserted, crumbling into rubble.

text.NRSV: they will not be rich, and their wealth will not endure, nor will they strike root in the earth;
text.Rotherham: He shall not be rich, nor shall his substance continue, neither shall their shadow stretch along on the earth;
text.KJV: 15:29 He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: He shall not be rich, his possessions shall not endure; his property shall not spread over the land.

text.NRSV: they will not escape from darkness; the flame will wither their shoots, and their blossom will be swept away by the wind.
text.Rotherham: He shall not depart out of darkness, his young branch, shall the flame dry up, and he shall depart, by the breath of his own mouth!
text.KJV: 15:30 He shall not depart out of darkenesse, the flame shall drie vp his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he goe away.
text.NABRE: A flame shall sear his early growth, and with the wind his blossoms shall disappear.

text.NRSV: Let them not trust in emptiness, deceiving themselves; for emptiness will be their recompense.
text.Rotherham: Let no one trust in him that—by vanity—is deceived, for, vanity, shall be his recompense;
text.KJV: 15:31 Let not him that is deceiued, trust in vanitie: for vanitie shalbe his recompence.
text.NABRE: Let him not trust in his height, misled, even though his height be like the palm tree.

text.NRSV: It will be paid in full before their time, and their branch will not be green.
text.Rotherham: Before his day, shall it be accomplished, with, his palm-top, not covered with leaves;
text.KJV: 15:32 It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not bee greene.
text.NABRE: He shall wither before his time, his branches no longer green.

text.NRSV: They will shake off their unripe grape, like the vine, and cast off their blossoms, like the olive tree.
text.Rotherham: He shall wrong—like a vine—his sour grapes, and shall cast off—as an olive-tree—his blossom.
text.KJV: 15:33 He shal shake off his vnripe grape as the Uine, and shall cast off his flowre as the Oliue.
text.NABRE: He shall be like a vine that sheds its grapes unripened, like an olive tree casting off its blossom.

text.NRSV: For the company of the godless is barren, and fire consumes the tents of bribery.
text.Rotherham: For, the family of the impious, is unfruitful, and, a fire, hath devoured the tents of bribery;
text.KJV: 15:34 For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of briberie.
text.NABRE: For the breed of the impious shall be sterile, and fire shall consume the tents of extortioners.

text.NRSV: They conceive mischief and bring forth evil and their heart prepares deceit.”
text.Rotherham: Conceiving mischief, and bringing forth iniquity, yea, their inmost soul, prepareth deceit.
text.KJV: 15:35 They conceiue mischiefe, and bring forth vanitie, and their belly prepareth deceit.
text.NABRE: They conceive malice, bring forth deceit, give birth to fraud.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 16:1 Then Iob answered, and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 Job’s Fourth Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are you all.
text.Rotherham: I have heard many such things, Wearisome comforters, are ye all!
text.KJV: 16:2 I haue heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.
text.NABRE: I have heard this sort of thing many times. Troublesome comforters, all of you!

text.NRSV: Have windy words no limit? Or what provokes you that you keep on talking?
text.Rotherham: Is there to be an end to windy words? Or what so strongly exciteth thee, that thou must respond?
text.KJV: 16:3 Shall vaine words haue an ende? or what emboldeneth thee, that thou answerest?
text.NABRE: Is there no end to windy words? What sickness makes you rattle on?

text.NRSV: I also could talk as you do, if you were in my place; I could join words together against you, and shake my head at you.
text.Rotherham: I also, like you, could speak,—If your soul were in the place of my soul, I could string together words against you, and could therewith shake over you my head.
text.KJV: 16:4 I also could speake as yee doe: if your soule were in my soules stead, I could heape vp words against you, and shake mine head at you.
text.NABRE: I also could talk as you do, were you in my place. I could declaim over you, or wag my head at you;

text.NRSV: I could fortify you with my mouth, and the solace of my lips would assuage your pain.
text.Rotherham: I could make you determined, by my mouth, and then my lip-solace should restrain you.
text.KJV: 16:5 But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the mouing of my lips should asswage your griefe.
text.NABRE: I could strengthen you with talk, with mere chatter give relief.

text.NRSV: “If I speak, my pain is not assuaged, and if I forbear, how much of it leaves me?
text.Rotherham: Though I do speak, unassuaged is my stinging pain,—And, if I forbear, of what am I relieved?
text.KJV: 16:6 Though I speake, my griefe is not asswaged: and though I forbeare; what am I eased?
text.NABRE: If I speak, my pain is not relieved; if I stop speaking, nothing changes.

text.NRSV: Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.
text.Rotherham: But, now, hath he wearied me, thou hast destroyed all my family;
text.KJV: 16:7 But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate al my companie.
text.NABRE: But now he has exhausted me; you have stunned all my companions.

text.NRSV: And he has shriveled me up, which has been a witness against me; my leanness has risen up against me, and it testifies to my face.
text.Rotherham: And, having captured me, it hath served, as a witness; and so my wasting away hath risen up against me, in my face, it answereth.
text.KJV: 16:8 And thou hast filled mee with wrinckles, which is a witnesse against me: and my leannesse rising vp in me, beareth witnesse to my face.
text.NABRE: You have shriveled me up; it is a witness, my gauntness rises up to testify against me;

text.NRSV: He has torn me in his wrath, and hated me; he has gnashed his teeth at me; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
text.Rotherham: His anger, hath torn and persecuted me, He hath gnashed upon me with his teeth, Mine adversary, hath sharpened his eyes for me.
text.KJV: 16:9 He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth vpon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes vpon me.
text.NABRE: His wrath tears and assails me, he gnashes his teeth against me; My enemy looks daggers at me.

text.NRSV: They have gaped at me with their mouths; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.
text.Rotherham: They have gaped upon me with their mouth, With reproach, have they smitten my cheek, Together, against me, have they closed their ranks.
text.KJV: 16:10 They haue gaped vpon me with their mouth, they haue smitten me vpon the cheeke reprochfully, they haue gathered themselues together against mee.
text.NABRE: They gape at me with their mouths; They strike me on the cheek with insults; they are all enlisted against me.

text.NRSV: God gives me up to the ungodly, and casts me into the hands of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: GOD doth abandon me to him that is perverse, and, into the hands of the lawless, he throweth me headlong.
text.KJV: 16:11 God hath deliuered me to the vngodly, and turned me ouer into the hands of the wicked.
text.NABRE: God has given me over to the impious; into the hands of the wicked he has cast me.

text.NRSV: I was at ease, and he broke me in two; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; he set me up as his target;
text.Rotherham: At ease, was I when he shattered me, Yea he seized me by my neck, and dashed me in pieces, then set me up for himself as a mark:
text.KJV: 16:12 I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my necke, and shaken me to pieces, and set me vp for his marke.
text.NABRE: I was in peace, but he dislodged me, seized me by the neck, dashed me to pieces. He has set me up for a target;
