text.NRSV: his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys, and shows no mercy; he pours out my gall on the ground.
text.Rotherham: His archers came round against me, He clave asunder my reins, and spared not, He poured out, on the earth, my gall:
text.KJV: 16:13 His archers compasse me round about, he cleaueth my reines asunder, and doeth not spare; he powreth out my gall vpon the ground.
text.NABRE: his arrows strike me from all directions. He pierces my sides without mercy, pours out my gall upon the ground.

text.NRSV: He bursts upon me again and again; he rushes at me like a warrior.
text.Rotherham: He made a breach in me, breach upon breach, He ran upon me, like a mighty man.
text.KJV: 16:14 He breaketh me with breach vpon breach, he runneth vpon me like a giant.
text.NABRE: He pierces me, thrust upon thrust, rushes at me like a warrior.

text.NRSV: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and have laid my strength in the dust.
text.Rotherham: Sackcloth, sewed I on my skin, and rolled—in the dust—my horn:
text.KJV: 16:15 I haue sowed sackcloth vpon my skin, and defiled my horne in the dust.
text.NABRE: I have sewn sackcloth on my skin, laid my horn low in the dust.

text.NRSV: My face is red with weeping, and deep darkness is on my eyelids,
text.Rotherham: My face, is reddened from weeping, and, upon mine eyelashes, is the death-shade:—
text.KJV: 16:16 My face is fowle with weeping, and on mine eye-lids is the shadow of death;
text.NABRE: My face is inflamed with weeping, darkness covers my eyes,

text.NRSV: though there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.
text.Rotherham: Though no violence was in my hands, and, my prayer, was pure.
text.KJV: 16:17 Not for any iniustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.
text.NABRE: Although my hands are free from violence, and my prayer sincere.

text.NRSV: “O earth, do not cover my blood; let my outcry find no resting place.
text.Rotherham: O earth! do not cover my blood, and let there be no place for mine outcry.
text.KJV: 16:18 O earth couer not thou my blood, and let my cry haue no place.
text.NABRE: O earth, do not cover my blood, nor let my outcry come to rest!

text.NRSV: Even now, see, my witness is in heaven, and he that vouches for me is on high.
text.Rotherham: Even now, lo! in the heavens, is my witness,
text.KJV: 16:19 Also now, behold my witnesse is in heauen, and my record is on high.
text.NABRE: Even now my witness is in heaven, my advocate is on high.

text.NRSV: My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God,
text.Rotherham: And, he that voucheth for me is on high. My friends are, they who scorn me, Unto GOD, hath mine eye shed tears:—
text.KJV: 16:20 My friends scorne me: but mine eye powreth out teares vnto God.
text.NABRE: My friends it is who wrong me; before God my eyes shed tears,

text.NRSV: that he would maintain the right of a mortal with God, as one does for a neighbor.
text.Rotherham: That one might plead, for a man, with GOD,—Even a son of man, for his friend!
text.KJV: 16:21 O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour.
text.NABRE: That justice may be done for a mortal with God: as for a man with his neighbor.

text.NRSV: For when a few years have come, I shall go the way from which I shall not return.”
text.Rotherham: When, a few years, come, then, by a path by which I shall not return, shall I depart.
text.KJV: 16:22 When a few yeeres are come, then I shall goe the way whence I shall not returne.
text.NABRE: For my years are numbered, and I go the road of no return.

text.NRSV: “My spirit is broken, my days are extinct, the grave is ready for me.
text.Rotherham: My spirit, is broken, My days, are extinguished, Graves, are left me.
text.KJV: 17:1 My breath is corrupt, my dayes are extinct, the graues are ready for me.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 My spirit is broken, my days finished, my burial at hand.

text.NRSV: Surely there are mockers around me, and my eye dwells on their provocation.
text.Rotherham: Verily there are mockers, with me! And, on their insults, mine eye doth rest.
text.KJV: 17:2 Are there not mockers with mee? and doeth not mine eye continue in their prouocation?
text.NABRE: Surely mockers surround me, at their provocation, my eyes grow dim.

text.NRSV: “Lay down a pledge for me with yourself; who is there that will give surety for me?
text.Rotherham: Appoint it, I pray thee—be thou surety for me with thyself, Who is there that, on my side, can pledge himself?
text.KJV: 17:3 Lay downe now, put me in a suretie with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
text.NABRE: Put up a pledge for me with you: who is there to give surety for me?

text.NRSV: Since you have closed their minds to understanding, therefore you will not let them triumph.
text.Rotherham: For, their heart, hast thou kept back from understanding, On this account, thou wilt not exalt them.
text.KJV: 17:4 For thou hast hid their heart from vnderstanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
text.NABRE: You darken their minds to knowledge; therefore you will not exalt them.

text.NRSV: Those who yield up their friends for a share of a possession—the eyes of their children will fail.
text.Rotherham: He that, for a share, denounceth friends, even, the eyes of his children, shall be dim.
text.KJV: 17:5 Hee that speaketh flattery to his friends, euen the eyes of his children shall faile.
text.NABRE: For a share of property he informs on friends, while the eyes of his children grow dim.

text.NRSV: “He has made me a byword of the peoples, and I am one before whom people spit.
text.Rotherham: But he hath set me, as the byword of peoples, And, one to be spit on in the face, do I become.
text.KJV: 17:6 He hath made me also a by-word of the people, and afore time I was as a tabret.
text.NABRE: I am made a byword of the people; I am one at whom people spit.

text.NRSV: My eye has grown dim from grief, and all my members are like a shadow.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath mine eye become dim from vexation, and, my members, are like a shadow, all of them.
text.KJV: 17:7 Mine eye also is dimme by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.
text.NABRE: My eyes are blind with anguish, and my whole frame is like a shadow.

text.NRSV: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent stir themselves up against the godless.
text.Rotherham: Upright men shall be astounded over this, and, the innocent, against the impious, shall rouse themselves.
text.KJV: 17:8 Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stirre vp himselfe against the hypocrite.
text.NABRE: The upright are astonished at this, the innocent aroused against the wicked.

text.NRSV: Yet the righteous hold to their way, and those with clean hands grow stronger and stronger.
text.Rotherham: That the righteous may hold on his way, and, the clean of hands, increase in strength.
text.KJV: 17:9 The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath cleane hands shalbe stronger, and stronger.
text.NABRE: The righteous holds to his way, the one with clean hands increases in strength.

text.NRSV: But you, come back now, all of you, and I shall not find a wise person among you.
text.Rotherham: But indeed, as for them all, will ye bethink yourselves and enter into it, I pray you? Or shall I not find, among you, one who is wise?
text.KJV: 17:10 But as for you all, doe you returne, and come now, for I cannot find one wise man among you.
text.NABRE: But turn now, and come on again; I do not find a wise man among you!

text.NRSV: My days are past, my plans are broken off, the desires of my heart.
text.Rotherham: My days, are past, my purposes, are broken off, the possessions of my heart!
text.KJV: 17:11 My dayes are past, my purposes are broken off, euen the thoughts of my heart:
text.NABRE: My days pass by, my plans are at an end, the yearning of my heart.

text.NRSV: They make night into day; ‘The light,’ they say, ‘is near to the darkness.’
text.Rotherham: Night for day, they appoint, Light, is near, by reason of darkness!
text.KJV: 17:12 They change the night into day: the light is short, because of darknes.
text.NABRE: They would change the night into day; where there is darkness they talk of approaching light.

text.NRSV: If I look for Sheol as my house, if I spread my couch in darkness,
text.Rotherham: If I wait for hades as my house, in darkness, have spread out my couch;
text.KJV: 17:13 If I waite, the graue is mine house: I haue made my bedde in the darknesse.
text.NABRE: If my only hope is dwelling in Sheol, and spreading my couch in darkness,

text.NRSV: if I say to the Pit, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’
text.Rotherham: To corruption, have exclaimed, My father, thou! My mother! and My sister! to the worm.
text.KJV: 17:14 I haue said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worme, Thou art my mother, and my sister.
text.NABRE: If I am to say to the pit, “You are my father,” and to the worm “my mother,” “my sister,”

text.NRSV: where then is my hope? Who will see my hope?
text.Rotherham: Where then would be my hope? And, as for my blessedness, who should see it!
text.KJV: 17:15 And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
text.NABRE: Where then is my hope, my happiness, who can see it?

text.NRSV: Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?”
text.Rotherham: With me to hades, would they go down, If, wholly—into the dust, is the descent!
text.KJV: 17:16 They shall goe downe to the barres of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
text.NABRE: Will they descend with me into Sheol? Shall we go down together into the dust?

text.NRSV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Bildad the Shuhite, and said:—
text.KJV: 18:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 Bildad’s Second Speech. Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and then we will speak.
text.Rotherham: How long will ye make a perversion of words? Ye should understand, and, afterwards, we could speak.
text.KJV: 18:2 How long will it bee, ere you make an ende of words? Marke, and afterwards we will speake.
text.NABRE: When will you put an end to words? Reflect, and then we can have discussion.

text.NRSV: Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore are we accounted like beasts? or appear stupid, in thine eyes?
text.KJV: 18:3 Wherefore are wee counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?
text.NABRE: Why are we accounted like beasts, equal to them in your sight?

text.NRSV: You who tear yourself in your anger—shall the earth be forsaken because of you, or the rock be removed out of its place?
text.Rotherham: One tearing in pieces his own soul in his anger,—For thy sake, shall the earth be forsaken? or the rock be moved out of its place?
text.KJV: 18:4 He teareth himselfe in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rocke bee remooued out of his place?
text.NABRE: You who tear yourself in your anger— shall the earth be neglected on your account or the rock be moved out of its place?

text.NRSV: “Surely the light of the wicked is put out, and the flame of their fire does not shine.
text.Rotherham: Even the light of the lawless, shall go out,—Neither shall shine the flame of his fire;
text.KJV: 18:5 Yea, the light of the wicked shalbe put out, and the sparke of his fire shall not shine.
text.NABRE: Truly, the light of the wicked is extinguished; the flame of his fire casts no light.

text.NRSV: The light is dark in their tent, and the lamp above them is put out.
text.Rotherham: The light, hath darkened in his tent, Yea, his lamp above him, goeth out;
text.KJV: 18:6 The light shalbe darke in his tabernacle, and his candle shalbe put out with him.
text.NABRE: In his tent light is darkness; the lamp above him goes out.

text.NRSV: Their strong steps are shortened, and their own schemes throw them down.
text.Rotherham: The steppings of his strength are hemmed in, and his own counsel casteth him down;
text.KJV: 18:7 The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his owne counsell shall cast him downe.
text.NABRE: His vigorous steps are hemmed in, his own counsel casts him down.

text.NRSV: For they are thrust into a net by their own feet, and they walk into a pitfall.
text.Rotherham: For he is thrust into a net by his own feet, and, upon a trap, he marcheth;
text.KJV: 18:8 For hee is cast into a net by his owne feete, & he walketh vpon a snare.
text.NABRE: A net catches him by the feet, he wanders into a pitfall.

text.NRSV: A trap seizes them by the heel; a snare lays hold of them.
text.Rotherham: There catcheth him—by the heel—a gin, there holdeth him fast—a noose:
text.KJV: 18:9 The grinne shall take him by the heele, and the robber shall preuaile against him.
text.NABRE: A trap seizes him by the heel, a snare lays hold of him.

text.NRSV: A rope is hid for them in the ground, a trap for them on the path.
text.Rotherham: Concealed in the ground is a cord for him,—and a snare for him, on the path.
text.KJV: 18:10 The snare is laide for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
text.NABRE: A noose is hidden for him on the ground, a netting for him on the path.

text.NRSV: Terrors frighten them on every side, and chase them at their heels.
text.Rotherham: Round about, terrors have startled him, and have driven him to his feet.
text.KJV: 18:11 Terrours shall make him afraid on euery side, and shall driue him to his feete.
text.NABRE: On every side terrors frighten him; they harry him at each step.

text.NRSV: Their strength is consumed by hunger, and calamity is ready for their stumbling.
text.Rotherham: Let his strength be famished, and, calamity, be ready at his side;
text.KJV: 18:12 His strength shalbe hunger-bitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
text.NABRE: His strength is famished, disaster is ready at his side,

text.NRSV: By disease their skin is consumed, the firstborn of Death consumes their limbs.
text.Rotherham: Let it devour the members of his body, Let the firstborn of death devour his members;
text.KJV: 18:13 It shall deuoure the strength of his skinne: euen the first borne of death shall deuoure his strength.
text.NABRE: His skin is eaten to the limbs, the firstborn of Death eats his limbs.

text.NRSV: They are torn from the tent in which they trusted, and are brought to the king of terrors.
text.Rotherham: Uprooted, out of his tent, be his confidence, and let it drive him down to the king of terrors;
text.KJV: 18:14 His confidence shalbe rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrours.
text.NABRE: He is plucked from the security of his tent; and marched off to the king of terrors.

text.NRSV: In their tents nothing remains; sulfur is scattered upon their habitations.
text.Rotherham: There shall dwell in his tent, what is naught-of-his, Let brimstone be strewed over his dwelling;
text.KJV: 18:15 It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because it is none of his: brimstone shall be scattered vpon his habitation.
text.NABRE: Fire lodges in his tent, over his abode brimstone is scattered.

text.NRSV: Their roots dry up beneath, and their branches wither above.
text.Rotherham: Beneath, let his roots be dried up, and, above, be cut off his branch;
text.KJV: 18:16 His rootes shall be dryed vp beneath: and aboue shall his branch be cut off.
text.NABRE: Below, his roots dry up, and above, his branches wither.

text.NRSV: Their memory perishes from the earth, and they have no name in the street.
text.Rotherham: His memorial, have perished out of the land, and let him have no name over the face of the open field;
text.KJV: 18:17 His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and hee shall haue no name in the streete.
text.NABRE: His memory perishes from the earth, and he has no name in the countryside.

text.NRSV: They are thrust from light into darkness, and driven out of the world.
text.Rotherham: Let them thrust him out of light into darkness, Yea, out of the world, let them chase him;
text.KJV: 18:18 He shall be driuen from light into darkenesse, and chased out of the world.
text.NABRE: He is driven from light into darkness, and banished from the world.

text.NRSV: They have no offspring or descendant among their people, and no survivor where they used to live.
text.Rotherham: Let him have neither scion nor seed among his people, neither any survivor in his place of sojourn:
text.KJV: 18:19 Hee shall neither haue sonne nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.
text.NABRE: He has neither offshoot nor offspring among his people, no survivor where once he dwelt.

text.NRSV: They of the west are appalled at their day, and horror seizes them of the east.
text.Rotherham: Over his day, have they been astounded who come behind, and, them who are in advance, a shudder hath seized.
text.KJV: 18:20 They that come after him shalbe astonied at his day, as they that went before, were affrighted.
text.NABRE: Those who come after shall be appalled at his fate; those who went before are seized with horror.

text.NRSV: Surely such are the dwellings of the ungodly, such is the place of those who do not know God.”
text.Rotherham: Surely, these, are the dwellings of him that is perverse, and, this, is the place of him that knoweth not GOD.
text.KJV: 18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God.
text.NABRE: So is it then with the dwelling of the impious; such is the place of the one who does not know God!

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 19:1 Then Iob answered, and sayd,
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Job’s Fifth Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “How long will you torment me, and break me in pieces with words?
text.Rotherham: How long will ye grieve my soul? or crush me with words?
text.KJV: 19:2 How long will yee vexe my soule, and breake me in pieces with words?
text.NABRE: How long will you afflict my spirit, grind me down with words?

text.NRSV: These ten times you have cast reproach upon me; are you not ashamed to wrong me?
text.Rotherham: These ten times, have ye reviled me, Shameless ye wrong me.
text.KJV: 19:3 These tenne times haue ye reproched me: you are not ashamed that you make your selues strange to me.
text.NABRE: These ten times you have humiliated me, have assailed me without shame!

text.NRSV: And even if it is true that I have erred, my error remains with me.
text.Rotherham: And even if indeed I have erred, with myself lodgeth mine error.
text.KJV: 19:4 And be it indeed that I haue erred, mine errour remaineth with my selfe.
text.NABRE: Even if it were true that I am at fault, my fault would remain with me;

text.NRSV: If indeed you magnify yourselves against me, and make my humiliation an argument against me,
text.Rotherham: If indeed, against me, ye must needs magnify yourselves, and plead, against me, my reproach.
text.KJV: 19:5 If indeed yee will magnifie your selues against me, and plead against me my reproch:
text.NABRE: If truly you exalt yourselves at my expense, and use my shame as an argument against me,

text.NRSV: know then that God has put me in the wrong, and closed his net around me.
text.Rotherham: Know, then, that, God, hath overthrown me, and, within his net, enclosed me.
text.KJV: 19:6 Know now that God hath ouerthrowen me, and hath compassed me with his net.
text.NABRE: Know then that it is God who has dealt unfairly with me, and compassed me round with his net.

text.NRSV: Even when I cry out, ‘Violence!’ I am not answered; I call aloud, but there is no justice.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I cry—out: Violence! but receive no answer, I cry aloud, but there is no vindication;
text.KJV: 19:7 Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloude, but there is no iudgement.
text.NABRE: If I cry out “Violence!” I am not answered. I shout for help, but there is no justice.

text.NRSV: He has walled up my way so that I cannot pass, and he has set darkness upon my paths.
text.Rotherham: My way, hath he walled up, that I cannot pass, and, upon my paths, hath he made darkness rest;
text.KJV: 19:8 Hee hath fenced vp my way that I cannot passe; and hee hath set darkenesse in my pathes.
text.NABRE: He has barred my way and I cannot pass; veiled my path in darkness;

text.NRSV: He has stripped my glory from me, and taken the crown from my head.
text.Rotherham: My glory—from off me, hath he stripped, and hath removed the crown of my head;
text.KJV: 19:9 Hee hath stript me of my glory, and taken the crowne from my head.
text.NABRE: He has stripped me of my glory, taken the diadem from my brow.

text.NRSV: He breaks me down on every side, and I am gone, he has uprooted my hope like a tree.
text.Rotherham: He hath ruined me on every side, and I am gone, and he hath taken away—like a tree—my hope;
text.KJV: 19:10 He hath destroyed me on euery side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he remooued like a tree.
text.NABRE: He breaks me down on every side, and I am gone; he has uprooted my hope like a tree.

text.NRSV: He has kindled his wrath against me, and counts me as his adversary.
text.Rotherham: Yea he hath kindled against me his anger, and accounted me towards him like unto his adversaries;
text.KJV: 19:11 He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and hee counteth me vnto him as one of his enemies.
text.NABRE: He has kindled his wrath against me; he counts me one of his enemies.

text.NRSV: His troops come on together; they have thrown up siegeworks against me, and encamp around my tent.
text.Rotherham: Together, enter his troops and have cast up, against me, their mound, and have encamped all around my tent;
text.KJV: 19:12 His troupes come together, and raise vp their way against me, and encampe round about my tabernacle.
text.NABRE: His troops advance as one; they build up their road to attack me, encamp around my tent.

text.NRSV: “He has put my family far from me, and my acquaintances are wholly estranged from me.
text.Rotherham: My Brethren—from beside me, hath he moved far away, and, mine acquaintance, are wholly estranged from me;
text.KJV: 19:13 Hee hath put my brethren farre from me, and mine acquaintance are verely estranged from me.
text.NABRE: My family has withdrawn from me, my friends are wholly estranged.

text.NRSV: My relatives and my close friends have failed me;
text.Rotherham: Failed me, have my near of kin, and, mine intimate acquaintances, have forgotten me;
text.KJV: 19:14 My kinsefolke haue failed, and my familiar friends haue forgotten me.
text.NABRE: My relatives and companions neglect me, my guests have forgotten me.

text.NRSV: the guests in my house have forgotten me; my serving maids count me as a stranger; I have become an alien in their eyes.
text.Rotherham: Ye guests of my house and my maidens, A stranger, have ye accounted me, An alien, have I become in their eyes;
text.KJV: 19:15 They that dwell in mine house, and my maides count me for a stranger: I am an aliant in their sight.
text.NABRE: Even my maidservants consider me a stranger; I am a foreigner in their sight.

text.NRSV: I call to my servant, but he gives me no answer; I must myself plead with him.
text.Rotherham: To mine own servant, I called, and he would not answer, With mine own mouth, I kept entreating him;
text.KJV: 19:16 I called my seruant, and he gaue me no answere: I intreated him with my mouth.
text.NABRE: I call my servant, but he gives no answer, though I plead aloud with him.

text.NRSV: My breath is repulsive to my wife; I am loathsome to my own family.
text.Rotherham: My breath, is strange to my wife, and I am loathsome to the sons of my own mother;
text.KJV: 19:17 My breath is strange to my wife, though I entreated for the childrens sake of mine owne body.
text.NABRE: My breath is abhorrent to my wife; I am loathsome to my very children.

text.NRSV: Even young children despise me; when I rise, they talk against me.
text.Rotherham: Even young children, despise me, I rise up, and they speak against me;
text.KJV: 19:18 Yea, yong children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
text.NABRE: Even young children despise me; when I appear, they speak against me.

text.NRSV: All my intimate friends abhor me, and those whom I loved have turned against me.
text.Rotherham: All the men of mine intimate circle abhor me, and, these whom I loved, have turned against me;
text.KJV: 19:19 All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loued, are turned against me.
text.NABRE: All my intimate friends hold me in horror; those whom I loved have turned against me!

text.NRSV: My bones stick to my skin and to my flesh, and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.
text.Rotherham: Unto my skin and unto my flesh, have my bones cleaved, and I have escaped with the akin of my teeth.
text.KJV: 19:20 My bone cleaueth to my skinne, and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skinne of my teeth.
text.NABRE: My bones cling to my skin, and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.

text.NRSV: Have pity on me, have pity on me, O you my friends, for the hand of God has touched me!
text.Rotherham: Pity me! pity me! ye, my friends, for, the hand of GOD, hath stricken me!
text.KJV: 19:21 Haue pity vpon me, haue pitie vpon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.
text.NABRE: Pity me, pity me, you my friends, for the hand of God has struck me!

text.NRSV: Why do you, like God, pursue me? Why are you not satisfied with my flesh?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should ye persecute me as GOD? and, with my flesh, should not he satisfied?
text.KJV: 19:22 Why doe ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
text.NABRE: Why do you pursue me like God, and prey insatiably upon me?

text.NRSV: “O that my words were written down! O that they were inscribed in a book!
text.Rotherham: Oh, then, that my words, could be written, Oh that, in a record, they could be inscribed:
text.KJV: 19:23 Oh that my wordes were now written, oh that they were printed in a booke!
text.NABRE: Oh, would that my words were written down! Would that they were inscribed in a record:

text.NRSV: O that with an iron pen and with lead they were engraved on a rock forever!
text.Rotherham: That, with a stylus of iron and [with] lead, for all time—in the rock, they could be graven!
text.KJV: 19:24 That they were grauen with an iron pen and lead, in the rocke for euer.
text.NABRE: That with an iron chisel and with lead they were cut in the rock forever!

text.NRSV: For I know that my Redeemer lives, and that at the last he will stand upon the earth;
text.Rotherham: But, I, know that, my redeemer, liveth, and, as the Last over [my] dust, will he arise;
text.KJV: 19:25 For I know that my Redeemer liueth, and that he shall stand at the latter day, vpon the earth:
text.NABRE: As for me, I know that my vindicator lives, and that he will at last stand forth upon the dust.

text.NRSV: and after my skin has been thus destroyed, then in my flesh I shall see God,
text.Rotherham: And, though, after my skin is struck off, this [followeth], yet, apart from my flesh, shall I see GOD:
text.KJV: 19:26 And though after my skin, wormes destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:
text.NABRE: This will happen when my skin has been stripped off, and from my flesh I will see God:

text.NRSV: whom I shall see on my side, and my eyes shall behold, and not another. My heart faints within me!
text.Rotherham: Whom, I myself, shall see, on my side, and, mine own eyes, [shall] have looked upon, and not those of a stranger. Exhausted are my deepest desires in my bosom!
text.KJV: 19:27 Whom I shal see for my selfe, and mine eyes shall beholde, and not another, though my reines bee consumed within me.
text.NABRE: I will see for myself, my own eyes, not another’s, will behold him: my inmost being is consumed with longing.

text.NRSV: If you say, ‘How we will persecute him!’ and, ‘The root of the matter is found in him’;
text.Rotherham: Surely ye should say—Why should we persecute him? seeing, the root of the matter, is found in me.
text.KJV: 19:28 But ye should say, Why persecute we him? seeing the root of the matter is found in me.
text.NABRE: But you who say, “How shall we persecute him, seeing that the root of the matter is found in him?”

text.NRSV: be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishment of the sword, so that you may know there is a judgment.”
text.Rotherham: Be ye afraid—on your part—of the face of the sword, because, wrath, [bringeth] the punishments of the sword, to the end ye may know the Almighty.
text.KJV: 19:29 Bee ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that yee may know there is a iudgement.
text.NABRE: Be afraid of the sword for yourselves, for your anger is a crime deserving the sword; that you may know that there is a judgment.

text.NRSV: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Zophar the Naamathite, and said:—
text.KJV: 20:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and saide,
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 Zophar’s Second Speech. Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Pay attention! My thoughts answer me, because of my haste within me.
text.Rotherham: Not so, do my thoughts answer me, and to this end, is my haste within me:
text.KJV: 20:2 Therefore doe my thoughts cause mee to answere, and for this I make haste.
text.NABRE: So now my thoughts provide an answer for me, because of the feelings within me.

text.NRSV: I hear censure that insults me, and a spirit beyond my understanding answers me.
text.Rotherham: The correction meant to confound me, I must hear, but, the spirit—out of my understanding, will give me a reply.
text.KJV: 20:3 I haue heard the checke of my reproach, and the spirit of my vnderstanding causeth me to answere.
text.NABRE: A rebuke that puts me to shame I hear, and from my understanding a spirit gives me a reply.

text.NRSV: Do you not know this from of old, ever since mortals were placed on earth,
text.Rotherham: Knowest thou, this—from antiquity, from the placing of man upon earth:—
text.KJV: 20:4 Knowest thou not this of old, since man was placed vpon earth,
text.NABRE: Do you not know this: from of old, since human beings were placed upon the earth,

text.NRSV: that the exulting of the wicked is short, and the joy of the godless but for a moment?
text.Rotherham: That, the joy-shout of the lawless, is short, and, the rejoicing of the impious, for a moment?
text.KJV: 20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short, and the ioy of the hypocrite but for a moment?
text.NABRE: The triumph of the wicked is short and the joy of the impious but for a moment?

text.NRSV: Even though they mount up high as the heavens, and their head reaches to the clouds,
text.Rotherham: Though his elevation mount up to the heavens, and, his head, to the clouds, doth reach,
text.KJV: 20:6 Though his excellencie mount vp to the heauens, and his head reach vnto the clouds:
text.NABRE: Though his pride mount up to the heavens and his head reach to the clouds,

text.NRSV: they will perish forever like their own dung; those who have seen them will say, ‘Where are they?’
text.Rotherham: Like his own stubble, shall he utterly perish, They who had seen him, shall say, Where is he?
text.KJV: 20:7 Yet he shall perish for euer, like his owne doung: they which haue seene him, shall say, Where is he?
text.NABRE: Yet he perishes forever like the dung he uses for fuel, and onlookers say, “Where is he?”

text.NRSV: They will fly away like a dream, and not be found; they will be chased away like a vision of the night.
text.Rotherham: Like a dream, shall he fly away, and they shall not find him, yea he shall be chased away, as a vision of the night.
text.KJV: 20:8 He shall flie away as a dreame, and shall not be found: yea he shalbe chased away as a vision of the night.
text.NABRE: Like a dream he takes flight and cannot be found; he fades away like a vision of the night.

text.NRSV: The eye that saw them will see them no more, nor will their place behold them any longer.
text.Rotherham: The eye that hath scanned him, shall not do it again, neither, any more, shall his place behold him:
text.KJV: 20:9 The eye also which saw him, shall see him no more; neither shall his place any more behold him.
text.NABRE: The eye which saw him does so no more; nor shall his dwelling again behold him.

text.NRSV: Their children will seek the favor of the poor, and their hands will give back their wealth.
text.Rotherham: His children, shall seek the favour of the poor, and, his own hand, shall give back his wealth.
text.KJV: 20:10 His children shall seeke to please the poore, and his hands shall restore their goods.
text.NABRE: His sons will restore to the poor, and his hands will yield up his riches.

text.NRSV: Their bodies are full of youthful vigor, but it will lie down with them in the dust.
text.Rotherham: His bones, are full of youthful vigour, yet, with him—in the dust, shall it lie down.
text.KJV: 20:11 His bones are ful of the sinne of his youth, which shall ye downe with him in the dust.
text.NABRE: Though his bones are full of youthful vigor, it shall lie with him in the dust.

text.NRSV: “Though wickedness is sweet in their mouth, though they hide it under their tongue,
text.Rotherham: Though, a sweet taste in his mouth, be given by vice, though he hide it under his tongue;
text.KJV: 20:12 Though wickednes be sweet in his mouth, though hee hide it vnder his tongue;
text.NABRE: Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth, and he hides it under his tongue,

text.NRSV: though they are loath to let it go, and hold it in their mouth,
text.Rotherham: Though he spare it, and will not let it go, but retain it in the midst of his mouth,
text.KJV: 20:13 Though he spare it, and forsake it not, but keepe it stil within his mouth:
text.NABRE: Though he retains it and will not let it go but keeps it still within his mouth,

text.NRSV: yet their food is turned in their stomachs; it is the venom of asps within them.
text.Rotherham: His food, in his stomach, is changed, the gall of adders, within him!
text.KJV: 20:14 Yet his meate in his bowels is turned, it is the gall of Aspes within him.
text.NABRE: Yet in his stomach the food shall turn; it shall be venom of asps inside him.

text.NRSV: They swallow down riches and vomit them up again; God casts them out of their bellies.
text.Rotherham: Wealth, hath he swallowed, and hath vomited the same, Out of his belly, shall, GOD, drive it forth:
text.KJV: 20:15 He hath swallowed downe riches, and hee shall vomite them vp againe: God shall cast them out of his belly.
text.NABRE: The riches he swallowed he shall vomit up; God shall make his belly disgorge them.

text.NRSV: They will suck the poison of asps; the tongue of a viper will kill them.
text.Rotherham: The poison of adders, shall he suck, The tongue of the viper shall slay him;
text.KJV: 20:16 He shall sucke the poison of Aspes: the vipers tongue shall slay him.
text.NABRE: The poison of asps he shall drink in; the viper’s fangs shall slay him.

text.NRSV: They will not look on the rivers, the streams flowing with honey and curds.
text.Rotherham: Let him not see in the channels the flowings of torrents of honey and milk.
text.KJV: 20:17 Hee shall not see the riuers, the floods, the brookes of hony and butter.
text.NABRE: He shall see no streams of oil, no torrents of honey or milk.

text.NRSV: They will give back the fruit of their toil, and will not swallow it down; from the profit of their trading they will get no enjoyment.
text.Rotherham: In vain, he toiled, he shall not swallow, like wealth to be restored, in which he cannot exult!
text.KJV: 20:18 That which he laboured for, shall he restore, & shall not swallow it downe: according to his substance shall the restitution bee, and hee shall not reioyce therein.
text.NABRE: He shall give back his gains, never used; like his profit from trade, never enjoyed.

text.NRSV: For they have crushed and abandoned the poor, they have seized a house that they did not build.
text.Rotherham: For he hath oppressed—hath forsaken the poor, A house, hath he seized, which he cannot rebuild.
text.KJV: 20:19 Because hee hath oppressed, and hath forsaken the poore; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not:
text.NABRE: Because he has oppressed and neglected the poor, and stolen a house he did not build;

text.NRSV: “Because they knew no quiet in their bellies, in their greed they let nothing escape.
text.Rotherham: Surely he hath known no peace in his inmost mind,—With his dearest thing, shall he not get away:
text.KJV: 20:20 Surely he shall not feele quietnesse in his belly, hee shall not saue of that which he desired.
text.NABRE: For he has known no quiet in his greed, in his treasure he cannot save himself.

text.NRSV: There was nothing left after they had eaten; therefore their prosperity will not endure.
text.Rotherham: Nothing escaped his devouring greed,—For this cause, shall his prosperity not continue:
text.KJV: 20:21 There shall none of his meat be left, therefore shall no man looke for his goods.
text.NABRE: None of his survivors will consume it, therefore his prosperity shall not endure.

text.NRSV: In full sufficiency they will be in distress; all the force of misery will come upon them.
text.Rotherham: When his abundance is gone, he shall be in straits, All the power of distress, shall come upon him.
text.KJV: 20:22 In the fulnesse of his sufficiencie, he shalbe in straites: euery hand of the wicked shall come vpon him.
text.NABRE: When he has more than enough, distress shall be his, every sort of trouble shall come upon him.

text.NRSV: To fill their belly to the full God will send his fierce anger into them, and rain it upon them as their food.
text.Rotherham: It shall be that, to fill his belly, he will thrust at him the glow of his anger, and rain [it] upon him for his punishment.
text.KJV: 20:23 When he is about to fill his belly, God shall cast the furie of his wrath vpon him, and shall raine it vpon him while he is eating.
text.NABRE: When he has filled his belly, God shall send against him the fury of his wrath and rain down his missiles upon him.

text.NRSV: They will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze bow will strike them through.
text.Rotherham: He shall flee from the armour of iron,—There shall pierce him, a bow of bronze!
text.KJV: 20:24 He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steele shall strike him through.
text.NABRE: Should he escape an iron weapon, a bronze bow shall pierce him through;

text.NRSV: It is drawn forth and comes out of their body, the glittering point comes out of their gall; terrors come upon them.
text.Rotherham: He hath drawn it out, and it hath come forth out of his back,—yea the flashing arrow-head, out of his gall, There shall march on him—terrors:
text.KJV: 20:25 It is drawen, and commeth out of the body; yea the glistering sword commeth out of his gall; terrours are vpon him.
text.NABRE: The dart shall come out of his back, a shining point out of his gall-bladder: terrors fall upon him.

text.NRSV: Utter darkness is laid up for their treasures; a fire unfanned will consume them; it will devour what is left in their tent.
text.Rotherham: Every misfortune, is laid up for his treasures,—There shall consume, a fire, not blown up,—it shall destroy what remaineth in his tent:
text.KJV: 20:26 All darknesse shalbe hid in his secret places: a fire not blowen shall consume him; it shall goe ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Complete darkness is in store for his treasured ones; a fire unfanned shall consume him; any survivor in his tent shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: The heavens will reveal their iniquity, and the earth will rise up against them.
text.Rotherham: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity, and, the earth, be rising up against him:
text.KJV: 20:27 The heauen shall reueale his iniquitie: and the earth shall rise vp against him.
text.NABRE: The heavens shall reveal his guilt, and the earth rise up against him.

text.NRSV: The possessions of their house will be carried away, a flood will sweep them away on the day of God’s wrath.
text.Rotherham: The increase of his house shall vanish, melting away in the day of his anger.
text.KJV: 20:28 The increase of his house shall depart, and his goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.
text.NABRE: The flood shall sweep away his house, torrents in the day of God’s anger.

text.NRSV: This is the portion of the wicked from God, the heritage appointed for them by God.”
text.Rotherham: This, is the portion of the lawless man, from God, and the inheritance decreed him from the Mighty One.
text.KJV: 20:29 This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed vnto him by God.
text.NABRE: This is the portion of the wicked, the heritage appointed him by God.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 21:1 But Iob answered, and sayd,
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Job’s Sixth Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Listen carefully to my words, and let this be your consolation.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye patiently my words, and let this be your consolation:
text.KJV: 21:2 Heare diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.
text.NABRE: At least listen to my words, and let that be the consolation you offer.

text.NRSV: Bear with me, and I will speak; then after I have spoken, mock on.
text.Rotherham: Suffer me, that, I, may speak, and, after I have spoken, thou canst mock!
text.KJV: 21:3 Suffer me that I may speake, and after that I haue spoken, mocke on.
text.NABRE: Bear with me while I speak; and after I have spoken, you can mock!

text.NRSV: As for me, is my complaint addressed to mortals? Why should I not be impatient?
text.Rotherham: Did, I, unto man, make my complaint? Wherefore, then, should my spirit not be impatient?
text.KJV: 21:4 As for mee, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled?
text.NABRE: Is my complaint toward any human being? Why should I not be impatient?

text.NRSV: Look at me, and be appalled, and lay your hand upon your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Turn round to me, and be astonished, and lay hand on mouth!
text.KJV: 21:5 Marke mee, and be astonished, and lay your hand vpon your mouth.
text.NABRE: Look at me and be appalled, put your hands over your mouths.

text.NRSV: When I think of it I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
text.Rotherham: When I call to mind, then am I dismayed, and there seizeth my flesh a shuddering:—
text.KJV: 21:6 Euen when I remember, I am afraid, and trembling taketh holde on my flesh.
text.NABRE: When I think of it, I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.

text.NRSV: Why do the wicked live on, reach old age, and grow mighty in power?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore do, lawless men, live, advance in years, even wax mighty in power?
text.KJV: 21:7 Wherefore doe the wicked liue, become old, yea, are mightie in power?
text.NABRE: Why do the wicked keep on living, grow old, become mighty in power?

text.NRSV: Their offspring are established in their presence, and their descendants before their eyes.
text.Rotherham: Their seed, is established in their sight, along with them, yea their offspring, before their eyes;
text.KJV: 21:8 Their seede is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.
text.NABRE: Their progeny is secure in their sight; their offspring are before their eyes.

text.NRSV: Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.
text.Rotherham: Their houses, are at peace, without dread, neither is, the rod of GOD, upon them;
text.KJV: 21:9 Their houses are safe from feare, neither is the rod of God vpon them.
text.NABRE: Their homes are safe, without fear, and the rod of God is not upon them.

text.NRSV: Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and never miscarries.
text.Rotherham: His bull, covereth, and causeth not aversion, His cow safely calveth, and casteth not her young;
text.KJV: 21:10 Their bull gendreth and faileth not, their cow calueth, and casteth not her calfe.
text.NABRE: Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.

text.NRSV: They send out their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.
text.Rotherham: They send forth—like a flock—their young ones, and, their children, skip about for joy;
text.KJV: 21:11 They send foorth their little ones like a flocke, and their children dance.
text.NABRE: They let their young run free like sheep, their children skip about.

text.NRSV: They sing to the tambourine and lyre, and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
text.Rotherham: They rejoice aloud as [with] timbrel and lyre, and make merry to the sound of the pipe;
text.KJV: 21:12 They take the timbrell and harpe, and reioyce at the sound of the organe.
text.NABRE: They sing along with drum and lyre, and make merry to the sound of the pipe.

text.NRSV: They spend their days in prosperity, and in peace they go down to Sheol.
text.Rotherham: They complete, in prosperity, their days, and, in a moment to hades, they sink down.
text.KJV: 21:13 They spend their daies in wealth, and in a moment goe downe to the graue.
text.NABRE: They live out their days in prosperity, and tranquilly go down to Sheol.

text.NRSV: They say to God, ‘Leave us alone! We do not desire to know your ways.
text.Rotherham: Yet they said unto GOD, Depart from us, and, In the knowledge of thy ways, find we no pleasure.
text.KJV: 21:14 Therefore they say vnto God, Depart from vs: for we desire not the knowledge of thy wayes.
text.NABRE: Yet they say to God, “Depart from us, for we have no desire to know your ways!

text.NRSV: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? And what profit do we get if we pray to him?’
text.Rotherham: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? Or what shall we profit, that we should urge him?
text.KJV: 21:15 What is the Almightie, that wee should serue him? and what profite should we haue, if we pray vnto him?
text.NABRE: What is the Almighty that we should serve him? And what do we gain by praying to him?”

text.NRSV: Is not their prosperity indeed their own achievement? The plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! not in their own hand, is their welfare, The counsel of lawless men, is far from me!
text.KJV: 21:16 Loe, their good is not in their hand, the counsell of the wicked is farre from me.
text.NABRE: Their happiness is not in their own hands. The designs of the wicked are far from me!

text.NRSV: “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does calamity come upon them? How often does God distribute pains in his anger?
text.Rotherham: How oft, the lamp of the lawless, goeth out, and their calamity, cometh upon them, Sorrows, apportioneth he in his anger;
text.KJV: 21:17 How oft is the candle of the wicked put out? and how oft commeth their destruction vpon them? God distributeth sorrowes in his anger.
text.NABRE: How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does destruction come upon them, the portion God allots in his anger?

text.NRSV: How often are they like straw before the wind, and like chaff that the storm carries away?
text.Rotherham: They become as straw before the wind, and as chaff, which the storm stealeth away.
text.KJV: 21:18 They are as stubble before the winde, and as chaffe that the storme carieth away.
text.NABRE: Let them be like straw before the wind, like chaff the storm carries away!

text.NRSV: You say, ‘God stores up their iniquity for their children.’ Let it be paid out to them and let them know it.
text.Rotherham: Shall, GOD, reserve, for his children, his sorrow? Let him recompense him so that he may know it;
text.KJV: 21:19 God layeth vp his iniquitie for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it.
text.NABRE: “God is storing up the man’s misery for his children”?— let him requite the man himself so that he knows it!

text.NRSV: Let their own eyes see their destruction, and let them drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
text.Rotherham: His own eyes, shall see his misfortune, and, the wrath of the Almighty, shall he drink.
text.KJV: 21:20 His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drinke of the wrath of the Almightie.
text.NABRE: Let his own eyes behold his calamity, and the wrath of the Almighty let him drink!

text.NRSV: For what do they care for their household after them, when the number of their months is cut off?
text.Rotherham: For what shall be his pleasure in his house after him, when, the number of his months, is cut in twain?
text.KJV: 21:21 For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his moneths is cut off in the middest?
text.NABRE: For what interest has he in his family after him, when the number of his months is finished?

text.NRSV: Will any teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those that are on high?
text.Rotherham: Is it, to GOD, one can teach knowledge, seeing that, he, shall judge, them who are on high?
text.KJV: 21:22 Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he iudgeth those that are high.
text.NABRE: Can anyone teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those on high?

text.NRSV: One dies in full prosperity, being wholly at ease and secure,
text.Rotherham: This, man dieth, in the very perfection of his prosperity, wholly tranquil and secure;
text.KJV: 21:23 One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.
text.NABRE: One dies in his full vigor, wholly at ease and content;

text.NRSV: his loins full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
text.Rotherham: His veins, are filled with nourishment, and, the marrow of his bones, is fresh;
text.KJV: 21:24 His breasts are full of milke, and his bones are moistened with marrow.
text.NABRE: His figure is full and nourished, his bones are moist with marrow.

text.NRSV: Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted of good.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, this other man, dieth, in bitterness of soul, and hath never tasted good fortune:
text.KJV: 21:25 And another dieth in the bitternesse of his soule, and neuer eateth with pleasure.
text.NABRE: Another dies with a bitter spirit, never having tasted happiness.

text.NRSV: They lie down alike in the dust, and the worms cover them.
text.Rotherham: Together, in the dust, they lie down, and, the worm, spreadeth a covering over them.
text.KJV: 21:26 They shall lie downe alike in the dust, and the wormes shall couer them.
text.NABRE: Alike they lie down in the dust, and worms cover them both.

text.NRSV: “Oh, I know your thoughts, and your schemes to wrong me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I know your plans, and the devices, wherewith ye would do me violence!
text.KJV: 21:27 Behold, I know your thoughts, and the deuices which yee wrongfully imagine against me.
text.NABRE: See, I know your thoughts, and the arguments you plot against me.

text.NRSV: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
text.Rotherham: For ye say, Where is the house of the noble-minded? And where the dwelling-tent of the lawless?
text.KJV: 21:28 For ye say, where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?
text.NABRE: For you say, “Where is the house of the great, and where the dwelling place of the wicked?”

text.NRSV: Have you not asked those who travel the roads, and do you not accept their testimony,
text.Rotherham: Have ye not asked the passers-by in the way? And, their signs, can ye not recognise?
text.KJV: 21:29 Haue ye not asked them that goe by the way? and doe ye not know their tokens?
text.NABRE: Have you not asked the wayfarers and do you not acknowledge the witness they give?

text.NRSV: that the wicked are spared in the day of calamity, and are rescued in the day of wrath?
text.Rotherham: That, to the day of calamity, is the wicked reserved, to the day of indignant visitation, are they led.
text.KJV: 21:30 That the wicked is reserued to the day of destruction; they shall bee brought foorth to the day of wrath.
text.NABRE: On the day of calamity the evil man is spared, on the day that wrath is released.

text.NRSV: Who declares their way to their face, and who repays them for what they have done?
text.Rotherham: Who can declare—to his face—his way? And, what, he, hath done, who shall recompense to him?
text.KJV: 21:31 Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
text.NABRE: Who will charge him to his face about his conduct, and for what he has done who will repay him?

text.NRSV: When they are carried to the grave, a watch is kept over their tomb.
text.Rotherham: Yet, he, to the graves, is borne, and, over the tomb, one keepeth watch;
text.KJV: 21:32 Yet shall hee be brought to the graue, & shall remaine in the tombe.
text.NABRE: He is carried to the grave and at his tomb they keep watch.

text.NRSV: The clods of the wadi are sweet to them; all people will follow after, and those who went before them are innumerable.
text.Rotherham: Pleasant to him are the mounds of the torrent-bed,—and, after him, doth every man march, as, before him, there were without number.
text.KJV: 21:33 The cloudes of the valley shalbe sweete vnto him, and euery man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.
text.NABRE: Sweet to him are the clods of the valley. All humankind will follow after him, and countless others before him.

text.NRSV: How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”
text.Rotherham: How then should ye comfort me with vanity, since, as for your replies, there lurketh, [in them] treachery?
text.KJV: 21:34 How then comfort ye me in vaine, seeing in your answeres there remaineth falshood?
text.NABRE: How empty the consolation you offer me! Your arguments remain a fraud.

text.NRSV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Eliphaz the Temanite, and said:—
text.KJV: 22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered, and said,
text.NABRE: IV. Third Cycle of Speeches Chapter 22 Eliphaz’s Third Speech. Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Can a mortal be of use to God? Can even the wisest be of service to him?
text.Rotherham: Unto GOD, can a man act as friend? Surely a discreet man befriendeth himself!
text.KJV: 22:2 Can a man be profitable vnto God? as hee that is wise may be profitable vnto himselfe.
text.NABRE: Can a man be profitable to God? Can a wise man be profitable to him?

text.NRSV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous, or is it gain to him if you make your ways blameless?
text.Rotherham: Is it a pleasure to the Almighty, that thou shouldst be righteous? or any profit, that thou shouldst be blameless in thy ways?
text.KJV: 22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or is it gaine to him, that thou makest thy waies perfite?
text.NABRE: Does it please the Almighty that you are just? Does he gain if your ways are perfect?

text.NRSV: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, and enters into judgment with you?
text.Rotherham: Is it, for thy reverence, that he will accuse thee? will enter with thee into judgment?
text.KJV: 22:4 Will hee reproue thee for feare of thee? will he enter with thee into iudgment?
text.NABRE: Is it because of your piety that he reproves you— that he enters into judgment with you?

text.NRSV: Is not your wickedness great? There is no end to your iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Is not, thy wickedness, great? and, without end, [are not] thine iniquities?
text.KJV: 22:5 Is not thy wickednesse great? and thine iniquities infinite?
text.NABRE: Is not your wickedness great, your iniquity endless?

text.NRSV: For you have exacted pledges from your family for no reason, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
text.Rotherham: Surely then hast been wont to put thy brother in pledge, for nothing, and, the garments of the ill-clad, hast thou stripped off:
text.KJV: 22:6 For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
text.NABRE: You keep your relatives’ goods in pledge unjustly, leave them stripped naked of their clothing.

text.NRSV: You have given no water to the weary to drink, and you have withheld food from the hungry.
text.Rotherham: No water—to the weary, hast thou given to drink, and, from the hungry, thou hast withheld bread:
text.KJV: 22:7 Thou hast not giuen water to the wearie to drinke, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.
text.NABRE: To the thirsty you give no water to drink, and from the hungry you withhold bread;

text.NRSV: The powerful possess the land, and the favored live in it.
text.Rotherham: A man of might, to him, pertaineth the land, and, the favorite, dwelleth therein:
text.KJV: 22:8 But as for the mightie man, hee had the earth, and the honourable man dwelt in it.
text.NABRE: As if the land belonged to the powerful, and only the privileged could dwell in it!

text.NRSV: You have sent widows away empty-handed, and the arms of the orphans you have crushed.
text.Rotherham: Widows, thou hast sent away empty, and, the arms of the fatherless, thou dost crush.
text.KJV: 22:9 Thou hast sent widowes away emptie, and the armes of the fatherlesse haue bene broken.
text.NABRE: You sent widows away empty-handed, and the resources of orphans are destroyed.

text.NRSV: Therefore snares are around you, and sudden terror overwhelms you,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, round about thee, are snares, and a dread startleth thee suddenly;
text.KJV: 22:10 Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden feare troubleth thee,
text.NABRE: Therefore snares are round about you, sudden terror makes you panic,

text.NRSV: or darkness so that you cannot see; a flood of water covers you.
text.Rotherham: Or darkness—thou canst not see, and, a flood of waters, covereth thee.
text.KJV: 22:11 Or darkenes that thou canst not see, and abundance of waters couer thee.
text.NABRE: Or darkness—you cannot see! A deluge of waters covers you.

text.NRSV: “Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars, how lofty they are!
text.Rotherham: Is not, GOD, [in] the height of the heavens? Behold, then, the head of the stars, that they are high.
text.KJV: 22:12 Is not God in the height of heauen? and behold the height of the starres how high they are.
text.NABRE: Does not God, in the heights of the heavens, behold the top of the stars, high though they are?

text.NRSV: Therefore you say, ‘What does God know? Can he judge through the deep darkness?
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou say then, What doth GOD know? Out through a thick cloud, can he judge?
text.KJV: 22:13 And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he iudge through the darke cloude?
text.NABRE: Yet you say, “What does God know? Can he judge through the thick darkness?

text.NRSV: Thick clouds enwrap him, so that he does not see, and he walks on the dome of heaven.’
text.Rotherham: Dark clouds, are a veil to him, and he cannot see, or, the vault of the heavens, doth he walk?
text.KJV: 22:14 Thicke cloudes are a couering to him that he seeth not, and hee walketh in the circuit of heauen.
text.NABRE: Clouds hide him so that he cannot see as he walks around the circuit of the heavens!”

text.NRSV: Will you keep to the old way that the wicked have trod?
text.Rotherham: The path of the ancient time, wilt thou mark, which the men of iniquity trod?
text.KJV: 22:15 Hast thou marked the olde way which wicked men haue troden?
text.NABRE: Do you indeed keep to the ancient way trodden by the worthless?

text.NRSV: They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away by a flood.
text.Rotherham: Who were snatched away before the time, and, a stream, washed away their foundation?
text.KJV: 22:16 Which were cut downe out of time, whose foundation was ouerflowen with a flood.
text.NABRE: They were snatched before their time; their foundations a river swept away.

text.NRSV: They said to God, ‘Leave us alone,’ and ‘What can the Almighty do to us?’
text.Rotherham: Who had been saying unto GOD, Depart from us! and—What can the Almighty do for himself?
text.KJV: 22:17 Which said vnto God, Depart from vs, and what can the Almightie doe for them?
text.NABRE: They said to God, “Let us alone!” and, “What can the Almighty do to us?”

text.NRSV: Yet he filled their houses with good things—but the plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
text.Rotherham: Yet, he, had filled their houses with good! The counsel of the lawless, then, is far from me:
text.KJV: 22:18 Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsell of the wicked is farre from me.
text.NABRE: Yet he had filled their houses with good things. The designs of the wicked are far from me!

text.NRSV: The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent laugh them to scorn, saying,
text.Rotherham: The righteous shall see and rejoice, and, the innocent, shall laugh them to scorn:
text.KJV: 22:19 The righteous see it, and are glad, and the innocent laugh them to scorne.
text.NABRE: The just look on and are glad, and the innocent deride them:

text.NRSV: ‘Surely our adversaries are cut off, and what they left the fire has consumed.’
text.Rotherham: If our assailants do not vanish, then, their abundance, a fire consumeth!
text.KJV: 22:20 Whereas our substance is not cut downe, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.
text.NABRE: “Truly our enemies are destroyed, and what was left to them, fire has consumed!”

text.NRSV: “Agree with God, and be at peace; in this way good will come to you.
text.Rotherham: Shew thyself to be one with him—I pray thee—and prosper, thereby, shall there come on thee blessing.
text.KJV: 22:21 Acquaint now thy selfe with him, and be at peace: thereby good shal come vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Settle with him and have peace. That way good shall come to you:

text.NRSV: Receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart.
text.Rotherham: Accept, I beseech thee, from his mouth—instruction,—and lay up his sayings in thy heart.
text.KJV: 22:22 Receiue, I pray thee, the Lawe from his mouth, and lay vp his words in thine heart.
text.NABRE: Receive instruction from his mouth, and place his words in your heart.

text.NRSV: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored, if you remove unrighteousness from your tents,
text.Rotherham: If thou return unto the Almighty and submit thyself, if thou far remove perversity from thy tent,
text.KJV: 22:23 If thou returne to the Almightie, thou shalt be built vp, thou shalt put away iniquitie farre from thy tabernacles.
text.NABRE: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored; if you put iniquity far from your tent,

text.NRSV: if you treat gold like dust, and gold of Ophir like the stones of the wadi,
text.Rotherham: Then lay up, in the dust, precious ore, and, among the stones of the torrent-beds, fine gold:
text.KJV: 22:24 Then shalt thou lay vp golde as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brookes.
text.NABRE: And treat raw gold as dust, the fine gold of Ophir as pebbles in the wadi,

text.NRSV: and if the Almighty is your gold and your chosen silver,
text.Rotherham: So shall, the Almighty, become, thy precious ores, yea glittering silver unto thee!
text.KJV: 22:25 Yea the Almightie shall bee thy defence, and thou shalt haue plenty of siluer.
text.NABRE: Then the Almighty himself shall be your gold and your sparkling silver.

text.NRSV: then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and lift up your face to God.
text.Rotherham: For, then, in the Almighty, shalt thou take exquisite delight, and shalt lift up—unto GOD—thy face;
text.KJV: 22:26 For then shalt thou haue thy delight in the Almightie, and shalt lift vp thy face vnto God.
text.NABRE: For then you shall delight in the Almighty, you shall lift up your face toward God.

text.NRSV: You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt make entreaty unto him, and he will hear thee, and, thy vows, shalt thou pay;
text.KJV: 22:27 Thou shalt make thy prayer vnto him, and he shall heare thee, and thou shalt pay thy vowes.
text.NABRE: Entreat him and he will hear you, and your vows you shall fulfill.

text.NRSV: You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt decree a purpose, and it shall be fulfilled unto thee, and, upon thy ways, shall have shone a light;
text.KJV: 22:28 Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shal be established vnto thee: and the light shall shine vpon thy wayes.
text.NABRE: What you decide shall succeed for you, and upon your ways light shall shine.

text.NRSV: For God abases the proud, but he saves the lowly.
text.Rotherham: When men cast themselves down, then thou shalt say: Up! And, him that is of downcast eyes, shall he save;
text.KJV: 22:29 When men are cast downe, then thou shalt say, There is lifting vp: and he shall saue the humble person.
text.NABRE: For when they are brought low, you will say, “It is pride!” But downcast eyes he saves.

text.NRSV: He will deliver even those who are not innocent; they will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
text.Rotherham: He shall deliver the innocent, and thou shalt escape by the pureness of thy hands.
text.KJV: 22:30 He shall deliuer the Iland of the innocent: and it is deliuered by the purenesse of thine hands.
text.NABRE: He will deliver whoever is innocent; you shall be delivered if your hands are clean.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 23:1 Then Iob answered, and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Job’s Seventh Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Today also my complaint is bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.
text.Rotherham: Even to-day, is my complaint rebellion? His hand, is heavier than my groaning.
text.KJV: 23:2 Euen to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heauier then my groning.
text.NABRE: Today especially my complaint is bitter, his hand is heavy upon me in my groanings.

text.NRSV: O that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his dwelling!
text.Rotherham: Oh that I knew where I might find him! I would come even unto his dwelling-place;
text.KJV: 23:3 O that I knewe where I might find him! that I might come euen to his seate!
text.NABRE: Would that I knew how to find him, that I might come to his dwelling!

text.NRSV: I would lay my case before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
text.Rotherham: I would set out, before him, a plea, and, my mouth, would I fill with arguments;
text.KJV: 23:4 I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
text.NABRE: I would set out my case before him, fill my mouth with arguments;

text.NRSV: I would learn what he would answer me, and understand what he would say to me.
text.Rotherham: I would note the words wherewith he would respond to me, and would mark what he would say to me.
text.KJV: 23:5 I would know the words which he would answere me, and vnderstand what he would say vnto me.
text.NABRE: I would learn the words he would answer me, understand what he would say to me.

text.NRSV: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; but he would give heed to me.
text.Rotherham: Would he, with fulness of might, contend with me? Nay, surely, he, would give heed to me!
text.KJV: 23:6 Will he plead against me with his great power? No, but hee would put strength in me.
text.NABRE: Would he contend against me with his great power? No, he himself would heed me!

text.NRSV: There an upright person could reason with him, and I should be acquitted forever by my judge.
text.Rotherham: There an upright man, might reason with him, so should I deliver myself completely from my judge.
text.KJV: 23:7 There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be deliuered for euer from my Iudge.
text.NABRE: There an upright man might argue with him, and I would once and for all be delivered from my judge.

text.NRSV: “If I go forward, he is not there; or backward, I cannot perceive him;
text.Rotherham: Behold! eastward, I go, but he is not there, and, westward, but I perceive him not;
text.KJV: 23:8 Behold, I goe forward, but he is not there, and backward, but I cannot perceiue him:
text.NABRE: But if I go east, he is not there; or west, I cannot perceive him;

text.NRSV: on the left he hides, and I cannot behold him; I turn to the right, and I cannot see him.
text.Rotherham: On the north, where he worketh, but I get no vision, He hideth himself on the south that I cannot see him.
text.KJV: 23:9 On the left hand where hee doeth worke, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himselfe on the right hand, that I cannot see him.
text.NABRE: The north enfolds him, and I cannot catch sight of him; The south hides him, and I cannot see him.

text.NRSV: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I shall come out like gold.
text.Rotherham: But, he, knoweth the way that I choose, Having tried me, as gold, I shall come forth.
text.KJV: 23:10 But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.
text.NABRE: Yet he knows my way; if he tested me, I should come forth like gold.

text.NRSV: My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
text.Rotherham: Of his steps, my foot taketh hold, His way, have I kept, and not swerved;
text.KJV: 23:11 My foot hath held his steps, his way haue I kept, and not declined.
text.NABRE: My foot has always walked in his steps; I have kept his way and not turned aside.

text.NRSV: I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured in my bosom the words of his mouth.
text.Rotherham: The command of his lips, and would not go back, and, in my bosom, have I treasured the words of his lips.
text.KJV: 23:12 Neither haue I gone backe from the commaundement of his lippes, I haue esteemed the words of his mouth more then my necessary food.
text.NABRE: From the commands of his lips I have not departed; the words of his mouth I have treasured in my heart.

text.NRSV: But he is unchangeable and who can turn him? What he desires, that he does.
text.Rotherham: But, he, is one, and who can turn him? What his soul desired, he hath done.
text.KJV: 23:13 But hee is in one minde, and who can turne him? and what his soule desireth, euen that he doeth.
text.NABRE: But once he decides, who can contradict him? What he desires, that he does.

text.NRSV: For he will complete what he appoints for me; and many such things are in his mind.
text.Rotherham: Surely he will accomplish what is decreed for me, and, many such things, hath he in store.
text.KJV: 23:14 For he performeth the thing that is appointed for mee: and many such things are with him.
text.NABRE: For he will carry out what is appointed for me, and many such things he has in store.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, from his presence, am I driven in fear, I diligently consider and am kept back from him in dread:
text.KJV: 23:15 Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
text.NABRE: Therefore I am terrified before him; when I take thought, I dread him.

text.NRSV: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
text.Rotherham: Yea, GOD, hath made timid my heart, and, the Almighty, hath put me in terror.
text.KJV: 23:16 For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:
text.NABRE: For it is God who has made my heart faint, the Almighty who has terrified me.

text.NRSV: If only I could vanish in darkness, and thick darkness would cover my face!
text.Rotherham: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, nor, before my face, did the gloom form a shroud.
text.KJV: 23:17 Because I was not cut off before the darknes, neither hath he couered the darknes from my face.
text.NABRE: Yes, would that I had vanished in darkness, hidden by the thick gloom before me.

text.NRSV: “Why are times not kept by the Almighty, and why do those who know him never see his days?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, since from the Almighty times are not hid, have, his knowing ones, no vision of his days?
text.KJV: 24:1 Why, seeing Times are not hidden from the Almightie, doe they, that know him not, see his dayes?
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 Why are times not set by the Almighty, and why do his friends not see his days?

text.NRSV: The wicked remove landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
text.Rotherham: Boundaries, men move back, flocks, they seize and consume;
text.KJV: 24:2 Some remooue the land-markes; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof.
text.NABRE: People remove landmarks; they steal herds and pasture them.

text.NRSV: They drive away the donkey of the orphan; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
text.Rotherham: The ass of the fatherless, they drive off, they take in pledge the ox of the widow;
text.KJV: 24:3 They driue away the asse of the fatherlesse, they take the widowes oxe for a pledge.
text.NABRE: The donkeys of orphans they drive away; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.

text.NRSV: They thrust the needy off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
text.Rotherham: They turn aside the needy out of the way, at once, are the humbled of the land made to hide themselves.
text.KJV: 24:4 They turne the needy out of the way: the poore of the earth hide themselues together.
text.NABRE: They force the needy off the road; all the poor of the land are driven into hiding.

text.NRSV: Like wild donkeys in the desert they go out to their toil, scavenging for food; the wasteland provides food for their children.
text.Rotherham: Lo! [as] wild asses in the wilderness, they go forth with their work, eager seekers for prey, the waste plain, yieldeth them food for their young;
text.KJV: 24:5 Behold, as wilde asses in the desart, goe they foorth to their worke, rising betimes for a pray: the wildernes yeeldeth food for them, and for their children.
text.NABRE: Like wild donkeys in the wilderness, they go forth to their task of seeking prey; the steppe provides food for their young;

text.NRSV: They reap in a field not their own and they glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: In the field—a man’s fodder, they cut down, and, the vineyard of the lawless, they strip of its late berries;
text.KJV: 24:6 They reape euery one his corne in the fielde: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.
text.NABRE: They harvest fodder in the field, and glean in the vineyard of the wicked.

text.NRSV: They lie all night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
text.Rotherham: Ill-clad, they are left to lodge without clothing, and have no covering in the cold;
text.KJV: 24:7 They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they haue no couering in the cold.
text.NABRE: They pass the night naked, without clothing; they have no covering against the cold;

text.NRSV: They are wet with the rain of the mountains, and cling to the rock for want of shelter.
text.Rotherham: With the sweeping rain of the mountains, are they wet, and, through having no shelter, they embrace a rock.
text.KJV: 24:8 They are wet with the showres of the mountaines, and imbrace the rocke for want of a shelter.
text.NABRE: They are drenched with rain from the mountains, and for want of shelter they cling to the rock.

text.NRSV: “There are those who snatch the orphan child from the breast, and take as a pledge the infant of the poor.
text.Rotherham: Men tear, from the breast, the fatherless, and, over the poor, they take a pledge;
text.KJV: 24:9 They plucke the fatherlesse from the brest, and take a pledge of the poore.
text.NABRE: Orphans are snatched from the breast, infants of the needy are taken in pledge.

text.NRSV: They go about naked, without clothing; hungry, they carry the sheaves;
text.Rotherham: Naked, they go about without clothing, and, famished, they carry the sheaves;
text.KJV: 24:10 They cause him to go naked without clothing: and they take away the sheafe from the hungry,
text.NABRE: They go about naked, without clothing, and famished, they carry the sheaves.

text.NRSV: between the terrace walls they press out oil; they tread the wine presses, but suffer thirst.
text.Rotherham: Between their walls, are they exposed to the sun, Wine-presses, they tread, and yet are thirsty;
text.KJV: 24:11 Which make oyle within their walles, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.
text.NABRE: Between the rows they press out the oil; they tread the wine presses, yet are thirsty.

text.NRSV: From the city the dying groan, and the throat of the wounded cries for help; yet God pays no attention to their prayer.
text.Rotherham: Out of the city—out of the houses, they make outcry. and, the soul of the wounded, calleth for help, and, GOD, doth not regard it as foolish.
text.KJV: 24:12 Men groane from out of the city, and the soule of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.
text.NABRE: In the city the dying groan, and the souls of the wounded cry out. Yet God does not treat it as a disgrace!

text.NRSV: “There are those who rebel against the light, who are not acquainted with its ways, and do not stay in its paths.
text.Rotherham: They, have become rebels against the light,—they are not acquainted with the ways thereof, neither abide they in the paths thereof.
text.KJV: 24:13 They are of those that rebell against the light, they know not the wayes thereof, nor abide in the pathes thereof.
text.NABRE: They are rebels against the light: they do not recognize its ways; they do not stay in its paths.

text.NRSV: The murderer rises at twilight to kill the poor and needy, and in the night is like a thief.
text.Rotherham: With the light, riseth the murderer, He slayeth the poor and needy, And, in the night, he becometh like a thief.
text.KJV: 24:14 The murderer rising with the light, killeth the poore and needy, and in the night is as a thiefe.
text.NABRE: When there is no light the murderer rises, to kill the poor and needy; in the night he acts like a thief.

text.NRSV: The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me’; and he disguises his face.
text.Rotherham: And, the eye of the adulterer, watcheth for the evening twilight, saying, Not an eye will see me! A covering for the face, he putteth on;
text.KJV: 24:15 The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face.
text.NABRE: The eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight; he says, “No eye will see me.” He puts a mask over his face;

text.NRSV: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
text.Rotherham: He breaketh, in the dark, into houses,—By day, they lock themselves in, They know not the light;
text.KJV: 24:16 In the darke they digge through houses which they had marked for themselues in the day time: they know not the light.
text.NABRE: in the dark he breaks into houses; By day they shut themselves in; they do not know the light.

text.NRSV: For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
text.Rotherham: For, in the case of all such, morning to them is the death-shade, For, to be recognised, is a death-shade terror.
text.KJV: 24:17 For the morning is to them euen as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrours of the shadow of death.
text.NABRE: Indeed, for all of them morning is deep darkness; then they recognize the terrors of deep darkness.

text.NRSV: “Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion in the land is cursed; no treader turns toward their vineyards.
