text.Rotherham: Swift is he on the face of the waters, Speedily vanished their share in the land, He turneth not to the way of the vineyards.
text.KJV: 24:18 Hee is swift as the waters, their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the Uineyards.
text.NABRE: He is swift on the surface of the water: their portion in the land is accursed, they do not turn aside by way of the vineyards.

text.NRSV: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
text.Rotherham: Drought and heat, steal away snow water, Hades, them who have sinned.
text.KJV: 24:19 Drought and heate consume the snow waters: so doeth the graue those which haue sinned.
text.NABRE: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters, Sheol, those who have sinned.

text.NRSV: The womb forgets them; the worm feeds sweetly on them; they are no longer remembered; so wickedness is broken like a tree.
text.Rotherham: Maternal love shall forget him, the worm shall find him sweet, No more shall he be remembered, but perversity shall be shivered like a tree.
text.KJV: 24:20 The wombe shall forget him, the worme shall feed sweetly on him, hee shall be no more remembred, and wickednes shalbe broken as a tree.
text.NABRE: May the womb forget him, may the worm find him sweet, may he no longer be remembered; And may wickedness be broken like a tree.

text.NRSV: “They harm the childless woman, and do no good to the widow.
text.Rotherham: He oppresseth the barren who beareth not, and, to the widow, he doeth not good;
text.KJV: 24:21 He euill intreateth the barren, that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.
text.NABRE: May his companion be barren, unable to give birth, may his widow not prosper!

text.NRSV: Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
text.Rotherham: Yea he draggeth along the mighty by his strength, He riseth up, and none hath assurance of life;
text.KJV: 24:22 He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth vp, and no man is sure of life.
text.NABRE: He sustains the mighty by his strength, to him who rises without assurance of his life

text.NRSV: He gives them security, and they are supported; his eyes are upon their ways.
text.Rotherham: It is given him to be secure, and confident, yet, his eyes, are upon their ways.
text.KJV: 24:23 Though it be giuen him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are vpon their wayes.
text.NABRE: he gives safety and support, and his eyes are on their ways.

text.NRSV: They are exalted for a little while, and then are gone; they wither and fade like the mallow; they are cut off like the heads of grain.
text.Rotherham: They are exalted a little, and are not, Yea having been laid low, like all men, are they gathered, Even as the top of an ear of corn, do they hang down.
text.KJV: 24:24 They are exalted for a litle while, but are gone and brought low, they are taken out of the way as al other, and cut off as the tops of the eares of corne.
text.NABRE: They are exalted for a while, and then are no more; laid low, like everyone else they are gathered up; like ears of grain they shrivel.

text.NRSV: If it is not so, who will prove me a liar, and show that there is nothing in what I say?”
text.Rotherham: But, if not, who then can convict me of falsehood? or make of no account my words?
text.KJV: 24:25 And if it be not so now, who will make mee a liar, and make my speach nothing worth?
text.NABRE: If this be not so, who can make me a liar, and reduce my words to nothing?

text.NRSV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Bildad the Shuhite, and said:—
text.KJV: 25:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said:
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Bildad’s Third Speech. Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:

text.NRSV: “Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
text.Rotherham: Dominion and dread, are with him, who causeth prosperity among his lofty ones;
text.KJV: 25:2 Dominion and feare are with him, hee maketh peace in his high places.
text.NABRE: Dominion and dread are his who brings about harmony in his heavens.

text.NRSV: Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
text.Rotherham: Is there any number to his troops? And upon whom ariseth not his light?
text.KJV: 25:3 Is there any number of his armies? and vpon whom doeth not his light arise?
text.NABRE: Is there any numbering of his troops? Yet on which of them does his light not rise?

text.NRSV: How then can a mortal be righteous before God? How can one born of woman be pure?
text.Rotherham: How then shall, a mortal, be just with GOD? Or how shall he be pure who is born of a woman?
text.KJV: 25:4 How then can man bee iustified with God? or how can he be cleane that is borne of a woman?
text.NABRE: How can anyone be in the right against God, or how can any born of woman be innocent?

text.NRSV: If even the moon is not bright and the stars are not pure in his sight,
text.Rotherham: Look as far as the moon, and it is not clear, and, the stars, are not bright in his eyes!
text.KJV: 25:5 Behold euen to the moone, and it shineth not, yea the starres are not pure in his sight.
text.NABRE: Even the moon is not bright and the stars are not clean in his eyes.

text.NRSV: how much less a mortal, who is a maggot, and a human being, who is a worm!”
text.Rotherham: How much less a mortal who is a creeping thing? Or a son of the earth-born who is a worm?
text.KJV: 25:6 How much lesse man, that is a worme: and the sonne of man which is a worme?
text.NABRE: How much less a human being, who is but a worm, a mortal, who is only a maggot?

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: 26:1 But Iob answered and sayd,
text.NABRE: Chapter 26 Job’s Reply. Then Job answered and said:

text.NRSV: “How you have helped one who has no power! How you have assisted the arm that has no strength!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou given help to one of no-strength? given victory to an arm of no-power?
text.KJV: 26:2 Howe hast thou helped him that is without power? how sauest thou the arme that hath no strength?
text.NABRE: What help you give to the powerless, what strength to the feeble arm!

text.NRSV: How you have counseled one who has no wisdom, and given much good advice!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou given counsel to one of no-wisdom? or, effective wisdom, abundantly made known?
text.KJV: 26:3 How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisedome? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing, as it is?
text.NABRE: How you give counsel to one without wisdom; how profuse is the advice you offer!

text.NRSV: With whose help have you uttered words, and whose spirit has come forth from you?
text.Rotherham: Whom hast thou taught speech? Whose inspiration hath come from thee?
text.KJV: 26:4 To whom hast thou vttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
text.NABRE: With whose help have you uttered those words, whose breath comes forth from you?

text.NRSV: The shades below tremble, the waters and their inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: The shades, tremble, beneath the waters and their inhabitants;
text.KJV: 26:5 Dead things are formed from vnder the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: The shades beneath writhe in terror, the waters, and their inhabitants.

text.NRSV: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
text.Rotherham: Naked is hades before him, and there is no covering to destruction;
text.KJV: 26:6 Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no couering.
text.NABRE: Naked before him is Sheol, and Abaddon has no covering.

text.NRSV: He stretches out the north over the void, and hangs the earth upon nothing.
text.Rotherham: Who stretcheth out the north over emptiness, hangeth the earth upon nothingness;
text.KJV: 26:7 He stretcheth out the North ouer the emptie place, and hangeth the earth vpon nothing.
text.NABRE: He stretches out Zaphon over the void, and suspends the earth over nothing at all;

text.NRSV: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not torn under them.
text.Rotherham: Who bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not rent beneath them;
text.KJV: 26:8 Hee bindeth vp the waters in his thicke clouds, and the cloud is not rent vnder them.
text.NABRE: He binds up the waters in his clouds, yet the cloud is not split by their weight;

text.NRSV: He covers the face of the full moon, and spreads over it his cloud.
text.Rotherham: Who shutteth-in the face of the throne, he spreadeth over it his cloud;
text.KJV: 26:9 Hee holdeth backe the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud vpon it.
text.NABRE: He holds back the appearance of the full moon by spreading his clouds before it.

text.NRSV: He has described a circle on the face of the waters, at the boundary between light and darkness.
text.Rotherham: A, boundary, hath he encircled on the face of the waters, as far as where light ends in darkness;
text.KJV: 26:10 Hee hath compassed the waters with bounds, vntill the day and night come to an end.
text.NABRE: He has marked out a circle on the surface of the deep as the boundary of light and darkness.

text.NRSV: The pillars of heaven tremble, and are astounded at his rebuke.
text.Rotherham: The pillars of the heavens, are shaken, and are terrified at his rebuke:
text.KJV: 26:11 The pillars of heauen tremble, and are astonished at his reproofe.
text.NABRE: The pillars of the heavens tremble and are stunned at his thunderous rebuke;

text.NRSV: By his power he stilled the Sea; by his understanding he struck down Rahab.
text.Rotherham: By his strength, hath he excited the sea, and, by his skill, hath he shattered the Crocodile:
text.KJV: 26:12 Hee diuideth the sea with his power, and by his vnderstanding he smiteth through the proud.
text.NABRE: By his power he stilled Sea, by his skill he crushed Rahab;

text.NRSV: By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
text.Rotherham: By his spirit, hath he arched the heavens, His hand hath pierced the fleeing serpent.
text.KJV: 26:13 By his spirit he hath garnished the heauens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
text.NABRE: By his wind the heavens were made clear, his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.

text.NRSV: These are indeed but the outskirts of his ways; and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”
text.Rotherham: Lo! these, are the fringes of his way, and what a whisper of a word hath been heard of him! But, the thunder of his might, who could understand?
text.KJV: 26:14 Loe, these are parts of his waies, but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can vnderstand?
text.NABRE: Lo, these are but the outlines of his ways, and what a whisper of a word we hear of him: Who can comprehend the thunder of his power?

text.NRSV: Job again took up his discourse and said:
text.Rotherham: And Job again took up his measure, and said:—
text.KJV: 27:1 Moreouer Iob continued his parable, and sayd,
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 Job’s Reply. Job took up his theme again and said:

text.NRSV: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, and the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
text.Rotherham: As GOD liveth who hath taken away my right, even the Almighty, who hath embittered my soul;
text.KJV: 27:2 As God liueth, who hath taken away my iudgment, and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soule;
text.NABRE: As God lives, who takes away my right, the Almighty, who has made my life bitter,

text.NRSV: as long as my breath is in me and the spirit of God is in my nostrils,
text.Rotherham: All the while my inspiration is in me, and the spirit of GOD is in my nostrils,
text.KJV: 27:3 All the while my breath is in mee, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;
text.NABRE: So long as I still have life breath in me, the breath of God in my nostrils,

text.NRSV: my lips will not speak falsehood, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
text.Rotherham: Verily my lips shall not speak perversity, nor shall, my tongue, utter deceit.
text.KJV: 27:4 My lips shall not speake wickednesse, nor my tongue vtter deceit.
text.NABRE: My lips shall not speak falsehood, nor my tongue utter deceit!

text.NRSV: Far be it from me to say that you are right; until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
text.Rotherham: Far be it from me! that I should justify, you,—Even until I breathe my last, will I not let go mine integrity from me:
text.KJV: 27:5 God forbid that I should iustifie you: till I die, I will not remoue my integritie from me.
text.NABRE: Far be it from me to account you right; till I die I will not renounce my innocence.

text.NRSV: I hold fast my righteousness, and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
text.Rotherham: On my righteousness, have I taken fast hold, and will not give it up, My heart shall not reproach any of my days.
text.KJV: 27:6 My righteousnesse I hold fast, and will not let it goe: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I liue.
text.NABRE: My justice I maintain and I will not relinquish it; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.

text.NRSV: “May my enemy be like the wicked, and may my opponent be like the unrighteous.
text.Rotherham: Let mine enemy be a veritably lawless one! And, he that lifteth himself up against me, one veritably perverse!
text.KJV: 27:7 Let mine enemie be as the wicked, and he that riseth vp against me, as the vnrighteous.
text.NABRE: Let my enemy be as the wicked and my adversary as the unjust!

text.NRSV: For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts them off, when God takes away their lives?
text.Rotherham: For what shall be the hope of the impious, though he graspeth with greed, when GOD shall draw forth his soul?
text.KJV: 27:8 For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soule?
text.NABRE: For what hope has the impious when he is cut off, when God requires his life?

text.NRSV: Will God hear their cry when trouble comes upon them?
text.Rotherham: His outcry, will GOD hear, when there cometh upon him distress?
text.KJV: 27:9 Will God heare his cry, when trouble commeth vpon him?
text.NABRE: Will God then listen to his cry when distress comes upon him,

text.NRSV: Will they take delight in the Almighty? Will they call upon God at all times?
text.Rotherham: Verily, in the Almighty, he will not find delight, nor call on GOD continually!
text.KJV: 27:10 Will he delight himselfe in the Almightie? will hee alwayes call vpon God?
text.NABRE: If he delights in the Almighty and calls upon God constantly?

text.NRSV: I will teach you about the hand of God; that which is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
text.Rotherham: I would teach you, by the hand of GOD, That which is with the Almighty, will I not conceal.
text.KJV: 27:11 I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almightie, will I not conceale.
text.NABRE: I will teach you what is in God’s hand, and the way of the Almighty I will not conceal.

text.NRSV: See, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
text.Rotherham: Lo! ye, have, all of you, seen, Wherefore, then, is it, that ye are utterly without purpose?
text.KJV: 27:12 Behold, all ye your selues haue seene it, why then are yee thus altogether vaine?
text.NABRE: Look, you yourselves have all seen it; why do you spend yourselves in empty words!

text.NRSV: “This is the portion of the wicked with God, and the heritage that oppressors receive from the Almighty:
text.Rotherham: This, is the portion of a lawless man with GOD, That, the heritage of tyrants—from the Almighty, he shall receive.
text.KJV: 27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressours which they shall receiue of the Almightie.
text.NABRE: This is the portion of the wicked with God, the heritage oppressors receive from the Almighty:

text.NRSV: If their children are multiplied, it is for the sword; and their offspring have not enough to eat.
text.Rotherham: If his children be multiplied, for them, [there is] the sword, and, his offspring, shall not be filled with bread;
text.KJV: 27:14 If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offpring shall not be satisfied with bread.
text.NABRE: Though his children be many, the sword awaits them. His descendants shall want for bread.

text.NRSV: Those who survive them are buried by pestilence, and their widows make no lamentation.
text.Rotherham: His survivors, by pestilence, shall come to the grave, and, his widows, shall not weep;
text.KJV: 27:15 Those that remaine of him shall bee buried in death: and his widowes shall not weepe.
text.NABRE: His survivors shall be buried in death; their widows shall not weep.

text.NRSV: Though they heap up silver like dust, and pile up clothing like clay—
text.Rotherham: Though he heap up silver like, dust, and, like a pile, he prepare clothing,
text.KJV: 27:16 Though he heape vp siluer as the dust, and prepare rayment as the clay:
text.NABRE: Though he heap up silver like dust and store away mounds of clothing,

text.NRSV: they may pile it up, but the just will wear it, and the innocent will divide the silver.
text.Rotherham: He may prepare, but, the righteous, shall put on, and, the silver, shall the innocent apportion.
text.KJV: 27:17 He may prepare it, but the iust shall put it on, and the innocent shall diuide the siluer.
text.NABRE: What he has stored the righteous shall wear, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

text.NRSV: They build their houses like a moth’s, like a booth that a watchman makes.
text.Rotherham: He hath built, like a moth, his house,—like a hut, which a watcher hath made.
text.KJV: 27:18 He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.
text.NABRE: He builds his house as of cobwebs, or like a booth put up by a watchman.

text.NRSV: They go to bed wealth, but will do so no more; they open their eyes, and it is gone.
text.Rotherham: The rich man, shall lie down, and not do it again, his eyes, hath he opened, and then is not.
text.KJV: 27:19 The rich man shall lie downe, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not:
text.NABRE: He lies down a rich man, one last time; he opens his eyes—nothing is there.

text.NRSV: Terrors overtake them like a flood; in the night a tempest carries them off.
text.Rotherham: There shall reach him—like waters—terrors, By night, a storm-wind hath stolen him away;
text.KJV: 27:20 Terrours take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.
text.NABRE: Terrors flood over him like water, at night the tempest carries him off.

text.NRSV: The east wind lifts them up and they are gone; it sweeps them out of their place.
text.Rotherham: An east wind shall lift him up, and he shall depart, and it shall sweep him away out of his place;
text.KJV: 27:21 The East winde carieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storme hurleth him out of his place.
text.NABRE: The east wind seizes him and he is gone; it sweeps him from his place;

text.NRSV: It hurls at them without pity; they flee from its power in headlong flight.
text.Rotherham: And He will cast upon him and not spare, Out of his hand, shall he, swiftly flee;
text.KJV: 27:22 For God shall cast vpon him, and not spare: hee would faine flee out of his hand.
text.NABRE: It hurls itself at him without pity, as he tries to flee from its power.

text.NRSV: It claps its hands at them, and hisses at them from its place.
text.Rotherham: He shall clap over him his hands, and shall hiss him forth out of his place.
text.KJV: 27:23 Men shall clap their handes at him, and shall hisse him out of his place.
text.NABRE: It claps its hands at him, and whistles at him from its place.

text.NRSV: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold to be refined.
text.Rotherham: Though there is, for silver, a vein, and a place for the gold they refine;
text.KJV: 28:1 Surely there is a veine for the siluer, and a place for golde where they fine it.
text.NABRE: V. The Poem on Wisdom Chapter 28 Where Is Wisdom to Be Found? There is indeed a mine for silver, and a place for refining gold.

text.NRSV: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.
text.Rotherham: Iron, out of the ore, is taken, and, stone, poureth out copper;
text.KJV: 28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth, and brasse is molten out of the stone.
text.NABRE: Iron is taken from the earth, and copper smelted out of stone.

text.NRSV: Miners put an end to darkness, and search out to the farthest bound the ore in gloom and deep darkness.
text.Rotherham: An end, hath one set to the darkness, and, into every extremity, is, he, making search, for the stone of darkness and death-shade;
text.KJV: 28:3 Hee setteth an ende to darkenesse, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkenesse and the shadow of death.
text.NABRE: He sets a boundary for the darkness; the farthest confines he explores.

text.NRSV: They open shafts in a valley away from human habitation; they are forgotten by travelers, they sway suspended, remote from people.
text.Rotherham: He hath sunken a shaft, away from the inhabitants; Places forsaken by the foot, they hang down, away from men, sway to and fro;
text.KJV: 28:4 The floud breaketh out from the inhabitant; euen the waters forgotten of the foote: they are dried vp, they are gone away from men.
text.NABRE: He breaks open a shaft far from habitation, unknown to human feet; suspended, far from people, they sway.

text.NRSV: As for the earth, out of it comes bread; but underneath it is turned up as by fire.
text.Rotherham: As for the earth, out of it, cometh forth bread, and, under it, is upturned, as it were fire;
text.KJV: 28:5 As for the earth, out of it commeth bread: and vnder it, is turned vp as it were fire.
text.NABRE: The earth, though out of it comes forth bread, is in fiery upheaval underneath.

text.NRSV: Its stones are the place of sapphires, and its dust contains gold.
text.Rotherham: The place of sapphires, are the stones thereof, and it hath, nuggets of gold:—
text.KJV: 28:6 The stones of it are the place of Saphires: and it hath dust of golde.
text.NABRE: Its stones are the source of lapis lazuli, and there is gold in its dust.

text.NRSV: “That path no bird of prey knows, and the falcon’s eye has not seen it.
text.Rotherham: A path, the vulture hath not discerned, nor hath the eye of the hawk scanned it;
text.KJV: 28:7 There is a path which no foule knoweth, and which the vulturs eye hath not seene.
text.NABRE: The path no bird of prey knows, nor has the hawk’s eye seen it.

text.NRSV: The proud wild animals have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
text.Rotherham: Ravenous beasts have not made a track thereof, neither hath the lion marched thereon:
text.KJV: 28:8 The lyons whelps haue not troden it, nor the fierce lyon passed by it.
text.NABRE: The proud beasts have not trodden it, nor has the lion gone that way.

text.NRSV: “They put their hand to the flinty rock, and overturn mountains by the roots.
text.Rotherham: Upon the flint, hath he thrust forth his hand, He hath turned up mountains by the roots;
text.KJV: 28:9 Hee putteth foorth his hand vpon the rocke; hee ouerturneth the mountaines by the rootes.
text.NABRE: He sets his hand to the flinty rock, and overturns the mountains at their root.

text.NRSV: They cut out channels in the rocks, and their eye sees every precious thing.
text.Rotherham: Among the rocks, hath he cut open streams, and, every precious thing, hath his eye seen:
text.KJV: 28:10 Hee cutteth out riuers among the rockes, and his eye seeth euery precious thing.
text.NABRE: He splits channels in the rocks; his eyes behold all that is precious.

text.NRSV: The sources of the rivers they probe; hidden things they bring to light.
text.Rotherham: From trickling, he restraineth, rivers, and, some hidden thing, is he bringing out to light.
text.KJV: 28:11 He bindeth the flouds from ouerflowing, and the thing that is hid, bringeth he foorth to light.
text.NABRE: He dams up the sources of the streams, and brings hidden things to light.

text.NRSV: “But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
text.Rotherham: Yet where can, Wisdom, be found? And where is the place of understanding?
text.KJV: 28:12 But where shall wisedome bee found? and where is the place of vnderstanding?
text.NABRE: As for wisdom—where can she be found? Where is the place of understanding?

text.NRSV: Mortals do not know the way to it, and it is not found in the land of the living.
text.Rotherham: Mortal knoweth not the way thereof, neither can it be found in the land of the living;
text.KJV: 28:13 Man knoweth not the price thereof neither is it found in the land of the liuing.
text.NABRE: Mortals do not know her path, nor is she to be found in the land of the living.

text.NRSV: The deep says, ‘It is not in me,’ and the sea says, ‘It is not with me.’
text.Rotherham: The resounding deep, hath said, It is not in me! And, the sea, hath said, It is not with me!
text.KJV: 28:14 The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.
text.NABRE: The Deep says, “She is not in me”; and the Sea says, “She is not with me.”

text.NRSV: It cannot be gotten for gold, and silver cannot be weighed out as its price.
text.Rotherham: Pure gold cannot be given in its stead, neither can silver he weighed as the value thereof;
text.KJV: 28:15 It cannot be gotten for golde, neither shall siluer be weighed for the price thereof.
text.NABRE: Solid gold cannot purchase her, nor can her price be paid with silver.

text.NRSV: It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, in precious onyx or sapphire.
text.Rotherham: It cannot be put into the scales against the gold of Ophir, with costly onyx, or sapphire;
text.KJV: 28:16 It cannot be valued with the golde of Ophir, with the precious Onix, or the Saphire.
text.NABRE: She cannot be bought with gold of Ophir, with precious onyx or lapis lazuli,

text.NRSV: Gold and glass cannot equal it, nor can it be exchanged for jewels of fine gold.
text.Rotherham: Neither gold nor crystal can compare with it, nor can, the exchange thereof, be a vessel of pure gold,
text.KJV: 28:17 The golde and the chrystall cannot equall it: and the exchange of it shall not be for iewels of fine golde.
text.NABRE: Gold or crystal cannot equal her, nor can golden vessels be exchanged for her.

text.NRSV: No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; the price of wisdom is above pearls.
text.Rotherham: Coral or crystal, cannot be mentioned, Yea, a possession, is wisdom, above red coral;
text.KJV: 28:18 No mention shalbe made of Corall, or of Pearles: for the price of wisedome is aboue Rubies.
text.NABRE: Neither coral nor crystal should be thought of; the value of wisdom surpasses pearls.

text.NRSV: The chrysolite of Ethiopia cannot compare with it, nor can it be valued in pure gold.
text.Rotherham: The topaz of Ethiopia cannot compare with it, Against purest gold, can it not be weighed.
text.KJV: 28:19 The Topaze of Ethiopia shall not equall it, neither shall it be valued with pure golde.
text.NABRE: Ethiopian topaz does not equal her, nor can she be weighed out for pure gold.

text.NRSV: “Where then does wisdom come from? And where is the place of understanding?
text.Rotherham: Whence then cometh, wisdom? And where is the place of understanding?
text.KJV: 28:20 Whence then commeth wisedome? and where is the place of vnderstanding?
text.NABRE: As for wisdom, where does she come from? Where is the place of understanding?

text.NRSV: It is hidden from the eyes of all living, and concealed from the birds of the air.
text.Rotherham: Seeing it hath been hid from the eyes of every living thing, and, from the bird of the heavens, hath it been concealed?
text.KJV: 28:21 Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all liuing, and kept close from the foules of the ayre.
text.NABRE: She is hidden from the eyes of every living thing; even from the birds of the air she is concealed.

text.NRSV: Abaddon and Death say, ‘We have heard a rumor of it with our ears.’
text.Rotherham: Destruction and death, have said, With our ears, have we heard the report thereof!
text.KJV: 28:22 Destruction and death say, Wee haue heard the fame thereof with our eares.
text.NABRE: Abaddon and Death say, “Only by rumor have we heard of her.”

text.NRSV: “God understands the way to it, and he knows its place.
text.Rotherham: GOD, understandeth the way thereof, and, he, discerneth the place thereof;
text.KJV: 28:23 God vnderstandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.
text.NABRE: But God understands the way to her; it is he who knows her place.

text.NRSV: For he looks to the ends of the earth, and sees everything under the heavens.
text.Rotherham: For, he, unto the ends of the earth, directeth his look, under all the heavens, he seeth;
text.KJV: 28:24 For hee looketh to the endes of the earth, and seeth vnder the whole heauen:
text.NABRE: For he beholds the ends of the earth and sees all that is under the heavens.

text.NRSV: When he gave to the wind its weight, and apportioned out the waters by measure;
text.Rotherham: Making, for the wind, a weight, and, the waters, he proved by measure,
text.KJV: 28:25 To make the weight for the windes, and he weigheth the waters by measure.
text.NABRE: When he weighed out the wind, and measured out the waters;

text.NRSV: when he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the thunderbolt;
text.Rotherham: When he made, for the rain, a decree, and a way for the lightning of thunders,
text.KJV: 28:26 When hee made a decree for the raine, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:
text.NABRE: When he made a rule for the rain and a path for the thunderbolts,

text.NRSV: then he saw it and declared it; he established it, and searched it out.
text.Rotherham: Then, saw he it, and declared it, He settled it, yea also he searched it out;
text.KJV: 28:27 Then did he see it, and declare it, he prepared it, yea and searched it out.
text.NABRE: Then he saw wisdom and appraised her, established her, and searched her out.

text.NRSV: And he said to humankind, ‘Truly, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.’”
text.Rotherham: And said to the son of earth, Lo! the reverence of the Lord, that, is wisdom, and, to avoid evil, is understanding.
text.KJV: 28:28 And vnto man he said, Behold, the feare of the Lord, that is wisedome, and to depart from euill, is vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: And to mortals he said: See: the fear of the Lord is wisdom; and avoiding evil is understanding.

text.NRSV: Job again took up his discourse and said:
text.Rotherham: And Job again took up his measure, and said,
text.KJV: 29:1 Moreouer Iob continued his parable, and said,
text.NABRE: VI. Job’s Final Summary of His Cause Chapter 29 Job took up his theme again and said:

text.NRSV: “O that I were as in the months of old, as in the days when God watched over me;
text.Rotherham: Oh that it were with me as in the months of old, as in the days, when, GOD, used to watch over me;
text.KJV: 29:2 O that I were as in moneths past, as in the dayes when God preserued me.
text.NABRE: Oh, that I were as in the months past, as in the days when God watched over me:

text.NRSV: when his lamp shone over my head, and by his light I walked through darkness;
text.Rotherham: When his lamp shone over my head, by whose light, I could go through darkness;
text.KJV: 29:3 When his candle shined vpon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkenesse:
text.NABRE: While he kept his lamp shining above my head, and by his light I walked through darkness;

text.NRSV: when I was in my prime, when the friendship of God was upon my tent;
text.Rotherham: As I was, in the days of my prime, when, the intimacy of GOD, was over my tent;
text.KJV: 29:4 As I was in the dayes of my youth, when the secret of God was vpon my tabernacle:
text.NABRE: As I was in my flourishing days, when God sheltered my tent;

text.NRSV: when the Almighty was still with me, when my children were around me;
text.Rotherham: While yet the Almighty was with me, round about me, were my young men;
text.KJV: 29:5 When the Almightie was yet with me, when my children were about me:
text.NABRE: When the Almighty was still with me, and my children were round about me;

text.NRSV: when my steps were washed with milk, and the rock poured out for me streams of oil!
text.Rotherham: When my steps were bathed in milk, and, the rock, poured out beside me, rivulets of oil:
text.KJV: 29:6 When I washed my steps with butter, and the rocke powred me out riuers of oyle:
text.NABRE: When my footsteps were bathed in cream, and the rock flowed with streams of oil.

text.NRSV: When I went out to the gate of the city, when I took my seat in the square,
text.Rotherham: When I went out to the gate unto the city, in the open place, made ready my seat,
text.KJV: 29:7 When I went out to the gate, through the citie, when I prepared my seate in the street.
text.NABRE: Whenever I went out to the gate of the city and took my seat in the square,

text.NRSV: the young men saw me and withdrew, and the aged rose up and stood;
text.Rotherham: Young men saw me, and hid themselves, and, the aged, arose—they stood;
text.KJV: 29:8 The yong men saw me, and hid themselues: and the aged arose, and stood vp.
text.NABRE: The young men saw me and withdrew, and the elders rose up and stood;

text.NRSV: the princes refrained from talking, and laid their hands on their mouths;
text.Rotherham: Rulers, restrained speech, and, a hand, laid they on their mouth;
text.KJV: 29:9 The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.
text.NABRE: Officials refrained from speaking and covered their mouths with their hands;

text.NRSV: the voice of the nobles was hushed, and their tongues stuck to the roof of their mouths.
text.Rotherham: The voice of nobles, was hushed, and their tongue to their palate, did cleave;
text.KJV: 29:10 The Nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaued to the roofe of their mouth.
text.NABRE: The voice of the princes was silenced, and their tongues stuck to the roofs of their mouths.

text.NRSV: When the ear heard, it commended me, and when the eye saw, it approved;
text.Rotherham: When, the ear, heard, then it pronounced me happy, When, the eye, saw, then it bare me witness;
text.KJV: 29:11 When the eare heard mee, then it blessed me, and when the eye saw me, it gaue witnesse to me:
text.NABRE: The ear that heard blessed me; the eye that saw acclaimed me.

text.NRSV: because I delivered the poor who cried, and the orphan who had no helper.
text.Rotherham: Because I used to deliver the oppressed who was crying out for aid, the fatherless also, and him that had no helper;
text.KJV: 29:12 Because I deliuered the poore that cried, and the fatherlesse, and him that had none to helpe him.
text.NABRE: For I rescued the poor who cried out for help, the orphans, and the unassisted;

text.NRSV: The blessing of the wretched came upon me, and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.
text.Rotherham: The blessing of him that was ready to perish, upon me was wont to descend, and, the heart of the widow, caused I to sing for joy;
text.KJV: 29:13 The blessing of him that was readie to perish, came vpon me: and I caused the widowes heart to sing for ioy.
text.NABRE: The blessing of those in extremity came upon me, and the heart of the widow I made joyful.

text.NRSV: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and a turban.
text.Rotherham: Righteousness, I put on, and it clothed me, Like a robe and turban, was my, justice;
text.KJV: 29:14 I put on righteousnesse, and it clothed me: my iudgement was as a robe and a diademe.
text.NABRE: I wore my righteousness like a garment; justice was my robe and my turban.

text.NRSV: I was eyes to the blind, and feet to the lame.
text.Rotherham: Eyes, became I to the, blind, and, feet to the lame, was, I!
text.KJV: 29:15 I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.
text.NABRE: I was eyes to the blind, and feet to the lame was I.

text.NRSV: I was a father to the needy, and I investigated the cause of the unknown.
text.Rotherham: A father, was, I, to the needy, and, as for the cause which I knew not, I used to search it out;
text.KJV: 29:16 I was a father to the poore: and the cause which I knewe not, I searched out.
text.NABRE: I was a father to the poor; the complaint of the stranger I pursued,

text.NRSV: I broke the fangs of the unrighteous, and made them drop their prey from their teeth.
text.Rotherham: And I shivered the fangs of the perverse,—and, out of his teeth, I tare the prey.
text.KJV: 29:17 And I brake the iawes of the wicked, and pluckt the spoile out of his teeth.
text.NABRE: And I broke the jaws of the wicked man; from his teeth I forced the prey.

text.NRSV: Then I thought, ‘I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days like the phoenix;
text.Rotherham: Then said I, Like a stem, shall I grow old, yea, as the sand, shall I multiply days:
text.KJV: 29:18 Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiplie my dayes as the sand.
text.NABRE: I said: “In my own nest I shall grow old; I shall multiply years like the phoenix.

text.NRSV: my roots spread out to the waters, with the dew all night on my branches;
text.Rotherham: My root, is laid open to the waters, and, the dew, shall lodge for the night in my boughs;
text.KJV: 29:19 My roote was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night vpon my branch.
text.NABRE: My root is spread out to the waters; the dew rests by night on my branches.

text.NRSV: my glory was fresh with me, and my bow ever new in my hand.’
text.Rotherham: Mine honour, shall be young again with me, and, my bow, in my hand, be renewed.
text.KJV: 29:20 My glory was fresh in mee, and my bow was renewed in my hand.
text.NABRE: My glory is fresh within me, and my bow is renewed in my hand!”

text.NRSV: “They listened to me, and waited, and kept silence for my counsel.
text.Rotherham: To me, men hearkened, and waited, and kept silence for my counsel;
text.KJV: 29:21 Unto me men gaue eare, and waited, and kept silence at my counsell.
text.NABRE: For me they listened and waited; they were silent for my counsel.

text.NRSV: After I spoke they did not speak again, and my word dropped upon them like dew.
text.Rotherham: After I had spoken, they spake not again, and, upon them, used my speech to drop;
text.KJV: 29:22 After my words they spake not againe, & my speach dropped vpon them,
text.NABRE: Once I spoke, they said no more, but received my pronouncement drop by drop.

text.NRSV: They waited for me as for the rain; they opened their mouths as for the spring rain.
text.Rotherham: And they waited, as for rain, for me, and, their mouths, they opened wide for the spring-rain;
text.KJV: 29:23 And they waited for me as for the raine, and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter raine.
text.NABRE: They waited for me as for the rain; they drank in my words like the spring rains.

text.NRSV: I smiled on them when they had no confidence; and the light of my countenance they did not extinguish.
text.Rotherham: I laughed at them—they lost confidence, and, the light of my countenance, they suffered not to fail;
text.KJV: 29:24 If I laughed on them, they beleeued it not, and the light of my countenance they cast not downe.
text.NABRE: When I smiled on them they could not believe it; they would not let the light of my face be dimmed.

text.NRSV: I chose their way, and sat as chief, I lived like a king among his troops, like one who comforts mourners.
text.Rotherham: I chose out their way, and sat chief,—and abode, as king, in an army, as one who, to mourners, giveth comfort.
text.KJV: 29:25 I chose out their way, and sate chiefe, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.
text.NABRE: I decided their course and sat at their head, I lived like a king among the troops, like one who comforts mourners.

text.NRSV: “But now they make sport of me, those who are younger than I, whose fathers I would have disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.
text.Rotherham: But, now, they who are of fewer days than I, have poured derision upon me; whose fathers I refused—to set with the dogs of my flock.
text.KJV: 30:1 But nowe they that are yonger then I, haue mee in derision, whose fathers I would haue disdained to haue set with the dogs of my flocke.
text.NABRE: Chapter 30 But now they hold me in derision who are younger than I, Whose fathers I should have disdained to rank with the dogs of my flock.

text.NRSV: What could I gain from the strength of their hands? All their vigor is gone.
text.Rotherham: Even the strength of their hands, wherefore was it mine? Upon them, vigour was lost;
text.KJV: 30:2 Yea whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom olde age was perished?
text.NABRE: Such strength as they had meant nothing to me; their vigor had perished.

text.NRSV: Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry and desolate ground,
text.Rotherham: In want and hunger, they were lean,—who used to gnaw the dry ground, a dark night of desolation!
text.KJV: 30:3 For want and famine they were solitarie: flying into the wildernesse in former time desolate and waste:
text.NABRE: In want and emaciating hunger they fled to the parched lands: to the desolate wasteland by night.

text.NRSV: they pick mallow and the leaves of bushes, and to warm themselves the roots of broom.
text.Rotherham: Who used to pluck off the mallow by the bushes, with the root of the broom for their food;
text.KJV: 30:4 Who cut vp mallowes by the bushes, and Iuniper rootes for their meate.
text.NABRE: They plucked saltwort and shrubs; the roots of the broom plant were their food.

text.NRSV: They are driven out from society; people shout after them as after a thief.
text.Rotherham: Out of the midst, were they driven, men shouted after them, as after a thief;
text.KJV: 30:5 They were driuen foorth from among men, (they cried after them, as after a thiefe.)
text.NABRE: They were banished from the community, with an outcry like that against a thief—

text.NRSV: In the gullies of wadis they must live, in holes in the ground, and in the rocks.
text.Rotherham: In the fissures, of the ravines had they to dwell, in holes of dust and crags;
text.KJV: 30:6 To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, in caues of the earth, and in the rockes.
text.NABRE: To dwell on the slopes of the wadies, in caves of sand and stone;

text.NRSV: Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.
text.Rotherham: Among the bushes, used they to shriek, Under the bramble, were they huddled together:
text.KJV: 30:7 Among the bushes they brayed: vnder the nettles they were gathered together.
text.NABRE: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they huddled together.

text.NRSV: A senseless, disreputable brood, they have been whipped out of the land.
text.Rotherham: Sons of the base, yea sons of the nameless, they were scourged out of the land.
text.KJV: 30:8 They were children of fooles, yea children of base men: they were viler then the earth.
text.NABRE: Irresponsible, of no account, they were driven out of the land.

text.NRSV: “And now they mock me in song; I am a byword to them.
text.Rotherham: But, now, their song, have I become, Yea I serve them for a byword;
text.KJV: 30:9 And now am I their song, yea I am their by-word.
text.NABRE: Yet now they sing of me in mockery; I have become a byword among them.

text.NRSV: They abhor me, they keep aloof from me; they do not hesitate to spit at the sight of me.
text.Rotherham: They abhor me—have put themselves far from me, and, from my face, have not withheld—spittle!
text.KJV: 30:10 They abhorre me, they flee farre from me, and spare not to spit in my face.
text.NABRE: They abhor me, they stand aloof, they do not hesitate to spit in my face!

text.NRSV: Because God has loosed my bowstring and humbled me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.
text.Rotherham: Because, my girdle, he had loosened and had humbled me, therefore, the bridle—in my presence, cast they off;
text.KJV: 30:11 Because hee hath loosed my cord and afflicted me, they haue also let loose the bridle before me.
text.NABRE: Because he has loosened my bowstring and afflicted me, they have thrown off restraint in my presence.

text.NRSV: On my right hand the rabble rise up; they send me sprawling, and build roads for my ruin.
text.Rotherham: On my right hand, the young brood rose up,—My feet, they thrust aside, and cast up against me their earthworks of destruction;
text.KJV: 30:12 Upon my right hand rise the youth, they push away my feete, and they raise vp against mee the wayes of their destruction.
text.NABRE: On my right the young rabble rise up; they trip my feet, they build their approaches for my ruin.

text.NRSV: They break up my path, they promote my calamity; no one restrains them.
text.Rotherham: They brake up my path,—My engulfing ruin, they helped forward, unaided;
text.KJV: 30:13 They marre my path, they set forward my calamitie, they haue no helper.
text.NABRE: They tear up my path, they promote my ruin, no helper is there against them.

text.NRSV: As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.
text.Rotherham: As through a wide breach, came they on, with a crashing noise, they rolled themselves along.
text.KJV: 30:14 They came vpon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselues vpon me.
text.NABRE: As through a wide breach they advance; amid the uproar they come on in waves;

text.NRSV: Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind, and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.
text.Rotherham: There are turned upon me terrors,—Chased away as with a wind, is mine abundance, and, as a cloud, hath passed away my prosperity.
text.KJV: 30:15 Terrours are turned vpon mee: they pursue my soule as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloude.
text.NABRE: terrors roll over me. My dignity is driven off like the wind, and my well-being vanishes like a cloud.

text.NRSV: “And now my soul is poured out within me; days of misery have taken hold of me.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, over myself, my soul poureth itself out, There seize me days of affliction:
text.KJV: 30:16 And now my soule is powred out vpon me: the dayes of affliction haue taken hold vpon me.
text.NABRE: And now my life ebbs away from me, days of affliction have taken hold of me.

text.NRSV: The night racks my bones, and the pain that gnaws me takes no rest.
text.Rotherham: Night, boreth, my bones, all over me,—and, my sinews, find no rest;
text.KJV: 30:17 My bones are pierced in mee in the night season: and my sinewes take no rest.
text.NABRE: At night he pierces my bones, my sinews have no rest.

text.NRSV: With violence he seizes my garment; he grasps me by the collar of my tunic.
text.Rotherham: Most effectually, is my skin disfigured,—Like the collar of my tunic, it girdeth me about:
text.KJV: 30:18 By the great force of my disease, is my garment changed: it bindeth mee about as the collar of my coat.
text.NABRE: With great difficulty I change my clothes, the collar of my tunic fits around my waist.

text.NRSV: He has cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.
text.Rotherham: He hath cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.
text.KJV: 30:19 He hath cast mee into the myre, and I am become like dust and ashes.
text.NABRE: He has cast me into the mire; I have become like dust and ashes.

text.NRSV: I cry to you and you do not answer me; I stand, and you merely look at me.
text.Rotherham: I cry out for help unto thee, and thou dost not answer, I stand still, and thou dost gaze at me;
text.KJV: 30:20 I crie vnto thee, and thou doest not heare me: I stand vp, and thou regardest me not.
text.NABRE: I cry to you, but you do not answer me; I stand, but you take no notice.

text.NRSV: You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.
text.Rotherham: Thou art turned to become a cruel one unto me, With the might of thy hand, thou assailest me;
text.KJV: 30:21 Thou art become cruell to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thy selfe against me.
text.NABRE: You have turned into my tormentor, and with your strong hand you attack me.

text.NRSV: You lift me up on the wind, you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.
text.Rotherham: Thou liftest up me to the wind, thou carriest me away, and the storm maketh me faint;
text.KJV: 30:22 Thou liftest me vp to the wind: thou causest me to ride vpon it, and dissoluest my substance.
text.NABRE: You raise me up and drive me before the wind; I am tossed about by the tempest.

text.NRSV: I know that you will bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.
text.Rotherham: For I know that, unto death, thou wilt bring me back, even unto the house of meeting for every one living.
text.KJV: 30:23 For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all liuing.
text.NABRE: Indeed I know that you will return me to death to the house destined for everyone alive.

text.NRSV: “Surely one does not turn against the needy, when in disaster they cry for help.
text.Rotherham: Only, against a heap of ruins, will one not thrust a hand! Surely, when one is in calamity—for that very reason, is there an outcry for help.
text.KJV: 30:24 Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the graue, though they cry in his destruction.
text.NABRE: Yet should not a hand be held out to help a wretched person in distress?

text.NRSV: Did I not weep for those whose day was hard? Was not my soul grieved for the poor?
text.Rotherham: Verily I wept, for him whose lot was hard, Grieved was my soul, for the needy.
text.KJV: 30:25 Did not I weepe for him that was in trouble? was not my soule grieued for the poore?
text.NABRE: Did I not weep for the hardships of others; was not my soul grieved for the poor?

text.NRSV: But when I looked for good, evil came; and when I waited for light, darkness came.
text.Rotherham: Surely, for good, I looked, but there came in evil, And I waited for light, but there came in darkness;
text.KJV: 30:26 When I looked for good, then euill came vnto mee: and when I waited for light, there came darkenes.
text.NABRE: Yet when I looked for good, evil came; when I expected light, darkness came.

text.NRSV: My inward parts are in turmoil, and are never still; days of affliction come to meet me.
text.Rotherham: I boiled within me, and rested not, There confronted me—days of affliction;
text.KJV: 30:27 My bowels boyled and rested not: the dayes of affliction preuented mee.
text.NABRE: My inward parts seethe and will not be stilled; days of affliction have overtaken me.

text.NRSV: I go about in sunless gloom; I stand up in the assembly and cry for help.
text.Rotherham: In gloom, I walked along, without sun, I arose—in the convocation, I cried out for help;
text.KJV: 30:28 I went mourning without the Sunne: I stood vp, and I cried in the Congregation.
text.NABRE: I go about in gloom, without the sun; I rise in the assembly and cry for help.

text.NRSV: I am a brother of jackals, and a companion of ostriches.
text.Rotherham: A brother, became I to the brutes that howl, and a companion to the birds that screech:
text.KJV: 30:29 I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owles.
text.NABRE: I have become a brother to jackals, a companion to ostriches.

text.NRSV: My skin turns black and falls from me, and my bones burn with heat.
text.Rotherham: My skin, turned black, and peeled off me, and, my bones, burned with heat:
text.KJV: 30:30 My skinne is blacke vpon mee, and my bones are burnt with heat.
text.NABRE: My blackened skin falls away from me; my very frame is scorched by the heat.

text.NRSV: My lyre is turned to mourning, and my pipe to the voice of those who weep.
text.Rotherham: Thus is attuned to mourning—my lyre, and my flute, to the noise of them who weep.
text.KJV: 30:31 My harpe also is turned to mourning, and my organe into the voyce of them that weepe.
text.NABRE: My lyre is tuned to mourning, and my reed pipe to sounds of weeping.

text.NRSV: “I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I look upon a virgin?
text.Rotherham: A covenant, I solemnised for mine eyes,—How then could I gaze upon a virgin?
text.KJV: 31:1 I made a couenant with mine eyes; why then should I thinke vpon a mayd?
text.NABRE: Chapter 31 I made a covenant with my eyes not to gaze upon a virgin.

text.NRSV: What would be my portion from God above, and my heritage from the Almighty on high?
text.Rotherham: Or what would have been my portion of GOD from above? Or what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
text.KJV: 31:2 For what portion of God is there from aboue? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
text.NABRE: What portion comes from God above, what heritage from the Almighty on high?

text.NRSV: Does not calamity befall the unrighteous, and disaster the workers of iniquity?
text.Rotherham: Is there not calamity, for the perverse? and misfortune, for the workers of iniquity?
text.KJV: 31:3 Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquitie?
text.NABRE: Is it not calamity for the unrighteous, and woe for evildoers?

text.NRSV: Does he not see my ways, and number all my steps?
text.Rotherham: Would, he, not see my ways? and of all my steps, take account?
text.KJV: 31:4 Doeth not he see my wayes, and count all my steps?
text.NABRE: Does he not see my ways, and number all my steps?

text.NRSV: “If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot has hurried to deceit—
text.Rotherham: Verily I walked not in falsity, nor did my foot haste unto deceit:—
text.KJV: 31:5 If I haue walked with vanitie, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;
text.NABRE: If I have walked in falsehood and my foot has hastened to deceit,

text.NRSV: let me be weighed in a just balance, and let God know my integrity!—
text.Rotherham: Let him weigh me in balances of righteousness,—and let GOD take note of mine integrity!
text.KJV: 31:6 Let me bee weighed in an euen ballance, that God may know mine integritie.
text.NABRE: Let God weigh me in the scales of justice; thus will he know my innocence!

text.NRSV: if my step has turned aside from the way, and my heart has followed my eyes, and if any spot has cleaved to my hands;
text.Rotherham: If my goings have swerved from the way,—and, after mine eyes, hath gone my heart, and, to my hands, hath adhered any stain,
text.KJV: 31:7 If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaued to my hands:
text.NABRE: If my steps have turned out of the way, and my heart has followed my eyes, or any stain clings to my hands,

text.NRSV: then let me sow, and another eat; and let what grows for me be rooted out.
text.Rotherham: Let me sow but, another, eat. And let, what I have springing up, be uprooted!
text.KJV: 31:8 Then let mee sow, and let another eate, yea let my off-spring be rooted out.
text.NABRE: Then may I sow, but another eat, and may my produce be rooted up!

text.NRSV: “If my heart has been enticed by a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor’s door;
text.Rotherham: If my heart hath been enticed unto a woman, or, by the door of my neighbour, I have lien in wait,
text.KJV: 31:9 If mine heart haue bene deceiued by a woman, or if I haue layde wait at my neighbours doore:
text.NABRE: If my heart has been enticed toward a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor’s door;

text.NRSV: then let my wife grind for another, and let others bow down upon her.
text.Rotherham: Let my wife, grind to another, and, over her, let others bend!
text.KJV: 31:10 Then let my wife grind vnto another, and let others bow downe vpon her.
text.NABRE: Then may my wife grind for another, and may others kneel over her!

text.NRSV: For that would be a heinous crime; that would be a criminal offense;
text.Rotherham: Surely that had been a shameful thing! and that an iniquity for the judges!
text.KJV: 31:11 For this is an heinous crime, yea, it is an iniquitie to bee punished by the Iudges.
text.NABRE: For that would be heinous, a crime to be condemned,

text.NRSV: for that would be a fire consuming down to Abaddon, and it would burn to the root all my harvest.
text.Rotherham: Surely, a fire, had that been, which, unto destruction, would have consumed, and, of all mine increase, had it torn up the root.
text.KJV: 31:12 For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would roote out all mine encrease.
text.NABRE: A fire that would consume down to Abaddon till it uprooted all my crops.

text.NRSV: “If I have rejected the cause of my male or female slaves, when they brought a complaint against me;
text.Rotherham: If I refused the right of my servant, or my handmaid, when they contended with me,
text.KJV: 31:13 If I did despise the cause of my man-seruant, or of my mayd-seruant, when they contended with me:
text.NABRE: Had I refused justice to my manservant or to my maidservant, when they had a complaint against me,

text.NRSV: what then shall I do when God rises up? When he makes inquiry, what shall I answer him?
text.Rotherham: What then could I have done when GOD rose up? And, when he visited, what could I have answered him?
text.KJV: 31:14 What then shall I do, when God riseth vp? and when hee visiteth, what shall I answere him?
text.NABRE: What then should I do when God rises up? What could I answer when he demands an account?

text.NRSV: Did not he who made me in the womb make them? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
text.Rotherham: Did not he who, in the womb, made me, make him? And is not he who formed us in the body one?
text.KJV: 31:15 Did not hee that made mee in the wombe, make him? and did not one fashion vs in the wombe?
text.NABRE: Did not he who made me in the belly make him? Did not the same One fashion us in the womb?

text.NRSV: “If I have withheld anything that the poor desired, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
text.Rotherham: If I withheld—from pleasure—the poor, or, the eyes of the widow, I dimmed;
text.KJV: 31:16 If I haue withhelde the poore from their desire, or haue caused the eyes of the widow to faile:
text.NABRE: If I have denied anything that the poor desired, or allowed the eyes of the widow to languish

text.NRSV: or have eaten my morsel alone, and the orphan has not eaten from it—
text.Rotherham: Or, used to eat my morsel alone, so that the fatherless did not eat thereof;
text.KJV: 31:17 Or haue eaten my morsell my selfe alone, and the fatherlesse hath not eaten thereof:
text.NABRE: While I ate my portion alone, with no share in it for the fatherless,

text.NRSV: for from my youth I reared the orphan like a father, and from my mother’s womb I guided the widow—
text.Rotherham: Surely, from my youth, he grew up to me, as to a father, and, from my birth, I acted as guide to her:
text.KJV: 31:18 (For from my youth hee was brought vp with me as with a father, and I haue guided her from my mothers wombe.)
text.NABRE: Though like a father he has reared me from my youth, guiding me even from my mother’s womb—

text.NRSV: if I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing, or a needy person without a covering,
text.Rotherham: If I saw one perishing for lack of clothing, or that the needy had no covering;
text.KJV: 31:19 If I haue seene any perish for want of cloathing, or any poore without couering:
text.NABRE: If I have seen a wanderer without clothing, or a poor man without covering,

text.NRSV: whose loins have not blessed me, and who was not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;
text.Rotherham: If his loins did not bless me, or if, with the fleece of my lambs, he did not warm himself;
text.KJV: 31:20 If his loynes haue not blessed me, and if hee were not warmed with the fleece of my sheepe:
text.NABRE: Whose limbs have not blessed me when warmed with the fleece of my sheep;

text.NRSV: if I have raised my hand against the orphan, because I saw I had supporters at the gate;
text.Rotherham: If I shook—against the fatherless—my hand, when I saw, in the gate, his need of my help,
text.KJV: 31:21 If I haue lift vp my hand against the fatherlesse, when I saw my helpe in the gate:
text.NABRE: If I have raised my hand against the innocent because I saw that I had supporters at the gate—

text.NRSV: then let my shoulder blade fall from my shoulder, and let my arm be broken from its socket.
text.Rotherham: Let, my shoulder, from the shoulder-blade, fall, and, my arm, from the upper bone, be broken;
text.KJV: 31:22 Then let mine arme fall from my shoulder-blade, and mine arme be broken from the bone.
text.NABRE: Then may my arm fall from the shoulder, my forearm be broken at the elbow!

text.NRSV: For I was in terror of calamity from God, and I could not have faced his majesty.
text.Rotherham: For, a dread unto me, was calamity from GOD, and, from his majesty, I could not escape.
text.KJV: 31:23 For destruction from God was a terrour to mee: and by reason of his highnesse, I could not endure.
text.NABRE: For I dread calamity from God, and his majesty will overpower me.

text.NRSV: “If I have made gold my trust, or called fine gold my confidence;
text.Rotherham: If I made gold my stay, and, to precious metal, said, My confidence!
text.KJV: 31:24 If I haue made golde my hope, or haue said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence:
text.NABRE: Had I put my trust in gold or called fine gold my security;

text.NRSV: if I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, or because my hand had gotten much;
text.Rotherham: If I rejoiced because great was my substance, and, an abundance, my hand had discovered;
text.KJV: 31:25 If I reioyced because my wealth was great, and because mine hand had gotten much:
text.NABRE: Or had I rejoiced that my wealth was great, or that my hand had acquired abundance—

text.NRSV: if I have looked at the sun when it shone, or the moon moving in splendor,
text.Rotherham: If I looked at the sun, when it flashed forth light, or at the moon, majestically marching along;
text.KJV: 31:26 If I beheld the Sunne when it shined, or the Moone walking in brightnesse:
text.NABRE: Had I looked upon the light as it shone, or the moon in the splendor of its progress,

text.NRSV: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my mouth has kissed my hand;
text.Rotherham: And befooled secretly was my heart, so that my hand kissed my mouth,
text.KJV: 31:27 And my heart hath bene secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:
text.NABRE: And had my heart been secretly enticed to blow them a kiss with my hand,

text.NRSV: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges, for I should have been false to God above.
text.Rotherham: That too, had been a judicial iniquity, For I should have been false to GOD, above.
text.KJV: 31:28 This also were an iniquitie to be punished by the Iudge: For I should haue denied the God that is aboue.
text.NABRE: This too would be a crime for condemnation, for I should have denied God above.

text.NRSV: “If I have rejoiced at the ruin of those who hated me, or exulted when evil overtook them—
text.Rotherham: If rejoiced in the misfortune of him that hated me, or exulted when calamity found him;—
text.KJV: 31:29 If I reioyced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lift vp my selfe when euill found him:
text.NABRE: Had I rejoiced at the destruction of my enemy or exulted when evil came upon him,

text.NRSV: I have not let my mouth sin by asking for their life with a curse—
text.Rotherham: Neither did I suffer my palate to sin, by asking, with a curse, for his life:
text.KJV: 31:30 (Neither haue I suffered my mouth to sinne by wishing a curse to his soule.)
text.NABRE: Even though I had not allowed my mouth to sin by invoking a curse against his life—

text.NRSV: if those of my tent ever said, ‘O that we might be sated with his flesh!’—
text.Rotherham: If the men of my household have not said, Oh for some of his flesh—we cannot get filled,
text.KJV: 31:31 If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! wee cannot be satisfied.
text.NABRE: Had not the men of my tent exclaimed, “Who has not been filled with his meat!”

text.NRSV: the stranger has not lodged in the street; I have opened my doors to the traveler—
text.Rotherham: Outside, the sojourner lodged not for the night, My doors—to the wayfarer, I threw open.
text.KJV: 31:32 The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doores to the trauailer.
text.NABRE: No stranger lodged in the street, for I opened my door to wayfarers—

text.NRSV: if I have concealed my transgressions as others do, by hiding my iniquity in my bosom,
text.Rotherham: If I covered, like Adam, my transgressions, by hiding in my bosom mine iniquity,
text.KJV: 31:33 If I couered my transgressions, as Adam: by hiding mine iniquitie in my bosome:
text.NABRE: Had I, all too human, hidden my sins and buried my guilt in my bosom

text.NRSV: because I stood in fear of the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and did not go out of doors—
text.Rotherham: Then let me be made to tremble at a great throng, yea let, the contempt of families, terrify me, so that, keeping silence, I shall not go out of the door!
text.KJV: 31:34 Did I feare a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrifie me: that I kept silence, and went not out of the doore?
text.NABRE: Because I feared the great multitude and the scorn of the clans terrified me— then I should have remained silent, and not come out of doors!

text.NRSV: “O that I had one to hear me! (Here is my signature! let the Almighty answer me!) O that I had the indictment written by my adversary!
text.Rotherham: Oh that I had one to hear me, Lo! my crossmark, May, the Almighty, answer me! And would that, a book, mine opponent had written!
text.KJV: 31:35 O that one would heare me! beholde, my desire is, that the Almightie would answere me, and that mine aduersary had written a booke.
text.NABRE: Oh, that I had one to hear my case: here is my signature: let the Almighty answer me! Let my accuser write out his indictment!

text.NRSV: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it on me like a crown;
text.Rotherham: Oh! would I not, upon my shoulder, lift it, or bind it as a crown upon me;
text.KJV: 31:36 Surely I would take it vpon my shoulder, and bind it as a crowne to me.
text.NABRE: Surely, I should wear it on my shoulder or put it on me like a diadem;

text.NRSV: I would give him an account of all my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
text.Rotherham: The number of my footsteps, I would declare to him, Like a noble, would I draw near to him.
text.KJV: 31:37 I would declare vnto him the number of my steps, as a prince would I goe neere vnto him.
text.NABRE: Of all my steps I should give him an account; like a prince I should present myself before him.

text.NRSV: “If my land has cried out against me, and its furrows have wept together;
text.Rotherham: If, against me, my ground used to cry out, and, together, my ridges did weep;
text.KJV: 31:38 If my land cry against me, or that the furrowes likewise thereof complaine:
text.NABRE: If my land has cried out against me till its furrows wept together;

text.NRSV: if I have eaten its yield without payment, and caused the death of its owners;
text.Rotherham: If, the strength thereof, I used to eat, without payment, and, the soul of the holders thereof, I made groan;
text.KJV: 31:39 If I haue eaten the fruits thereof without money, or haue caused the owners thereof to loose their life:
text.NABRE: If I have eaten its strength without payment and grieved the hearts of its tenants;

text.NRSV: let thorns grow instead of wheat, and foul weeds instead of barley.” The words of Job are ended.
text.Rotherham: Instead of wheat, let there come forth bramble, and, instead of barley, a bad-smelling weed! Ended are the words of Job.
text.KJV: 31:40 Let thistles grow in stead of wheat, and cockle in stead of barley. The words of Iob are ended.
text.NABRE: Then let the thorns grow instead of wheat and stinkweed instead of barley! The words of Job are ended.

text.NRSV: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
text.Rotherham: So these three men ceased to respond to Job, because, he, was righteous in their eyes.
text.KJV: 32:1 So these three men ceased to answere Iob, because he was righteous in his owne eyes.
text.NABRE: VII. Elihu’s Speeches Chapter 32 Then the three men ceased to answer Job, because in his own eyes he was in the right.

text.NRSV: Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became angry. He was angry at Job because he justified himself rather than God;
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Elihu, son of Barachel the Buzite of the family of Ram,—against Job, was kindled his anger, because he justified his own soul rather than God;
text.KJV: 32:2 Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu, the sonne of Barachel the Buzite, of the kinred of Ram: against Iob was his wrath kindled, because he iustified himselfe rather then God.
text.NABRE: But the anger of Elihu, son of Barachel the Buzite, of the clan of Ram, was kindled. He was angry with Job for considering himself rather than God to be in the right.

text.NRSV: he was angry also at Job’s three friends because they had found no answer, though they had declared Job to be in the wrong.
text.Rotherham: and, against his three friends, was kindled his anger,—because that they found not a response, and condemned God.
text.KJV: 32:3 Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled: because they had found no answere, and yet had condemned Iob.
text.NABRE: He was angry also with the three friends because they had not found a good answer and had not condemned Job.

text.NRSV: Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job, because they were older than he.
text.Rotherham: But, Elihu, had waited for Job with words, because the others were older than he.
text.KJV: 32:4 Now Elihu had waited till Iob had spoken: because they were elder then he.
text.NABRE: But since these men were older than he, Elihu bided his time before addressing Job.

text.NRSV: But when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouths of these three men, he became angry.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, when Elihu saw that there was no response in the mouth of the three men, then was kindled his anger.
text.KJV: 32:5 When Elihu saw that there was no answere in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.
text.NABRE: When, however, Elihu saw that there was no reply in the mouths of the three men, his wrath was inflamed.

text.NRSV:
Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite answered:
“I am young in years, and you are aged; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you.
text.Rotherham: So then Elihu, son of Barachel, the Buzite, responded and said:—Young, am I, whereas, ye, are aged, For this cause, I faltered, and feared—to shew my knowledge unto you:
text.KJV: 32:6 And Elihu the sonne of Barachel the Buzite answered and sayd: I am yong, and yee are very old, wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.
text.NABRE: So Elihu, son of Barachel the Buzite, answered and said: I am young and you are very old; therefore I held back and was afraid to declare to you my knowledge.

text.NRSV: I said, ‘Let days speak, and many years teach wisdom.’
text.Rotherham: I said, Days, should speak,—and, the multitude of years, should make known wisdom.
text.KJV: 32:7 I said, Dayes should speake, and multitude of yeeres should teach wisedome.
text.NABRE: I thought, days should speak, and many years teach wisdom!

text.NRSV: But it is the spirit in a mortal, the breath of the Almighty, that makes for understanding.
text.Rotherham: Yet surely, there is a spirit in men, and, the inspiration of the Almighty, giveth them understanding;
text.KJV: 32:8 But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almightie giueth them vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: But there is a spirit in human beings, the breath of the Almighty, that gives them understanding.

text.NRSV: It is not the old who are wise, nor the aged who understand what is right.
text.Rotherham: Great men, may not be wise, nor, elders, understand justice.
text.KJV: 32:9 Great men are not alwayes wise: neither doe the aged vnderstand iudgement.
text.NABRE: It is not those of many days who are wise, nor the aged who understand the right.

text.NRSV: Therefore I say, ‘Listen to me; let me also declare my opinion.’
text.Rotherham: Therefore, I said, Hearken unto me, I will show my knowledge—even I.
text.KJV: 32:10 Therfore I sayd, Hearken to me: I also will shew mine opinion.
text.NABRE: Therefore I say, listen to me; I also will declare my knowledge!

text.NRSV: “See, I waited for your words, I listened for your wise thoughts, while you searched out what to say.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I waited for your words, I kept giving ear for your reasons, until ye should search out what to say;
text.KJV: 32:11 Behold, I waited for your words: I gaue eare to your reasons, whilest you searched out what to say.
text.NABRE: Behold, I have waited for your words, have given ear to your arguments, as you searched out what to say.

text.NRSV: I gave you my attention, but there was in fact no one that confuted Job, and none of you that answered his arguments.
text.Rotherham: Yea, unto you, gave I diligent heed,—But lo! there was, for Job, nothing to convince, nor could you of you answer his speeches.
text.KJV: 32:12 Yea, I attended vnto you: and beholde, there was none of you that conuinced Iob, or that answered his words:
text.NABRE: Yes, I followed you attentively: And look, none of you has convicted Job, not one could refute his statements.

text.NRSV: Beware lest you say, ‘We have found wisdom; let God vanquish him, not a human.’
text.Rotherham: Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom. GOD, must put him to flight, not man.
text.KJV: 32:13 Lest ye should say, We haue found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.
text.NABRE: So do not say, “We have met wisdom; God can vanquish him but no mortal!”

text.NRSV: He has not directed his words against me, and I will not answer him with your speeches.
text.Rotherham: Since he directed not to me discourse, therefore, with your speeches, will I not reply to him.
text.KJV: 32:14 Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answere him with your speeches.
text.NABRE: For had he addressed his words to me, I would not then have answered him with your words.

text.NRSV: “They are dismayed, they answer no more; they have not a word to say.
text.Rotherham: They were dismayed, they responded no more, they suffered speech to forsake them;
text.KJV: 32:15 They were amased, they answered no more, they left off speaking.
text.NABRE: They are dismayed, they make no more reply; words fail them.

text.NRSV: And am I to wait, because they do not speak, because they stand there, and answer no more?
text.Rotherham: Though I waited, yet could they not speak, Surely they came to a stand, they responded no more.
text.KJV: 32:16 When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still and answered no more.)
text.NABRE: Must I wait? Now that they speak no more, and have ceased to make reply,

text.NRSV: I also will give my answer; I also will declare my opinion.
text.Rotherham: I will respond, even I—on my part, I will shew my knowledge, even I!
text.KJV: 32:17 I sayd, I will answere also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.
text.NABRE: I too will speak my part; I also will declare my knowledge!

text.NRSV: For I am full of words; the spirit within me constrains me.
text.Rotherham: For I am full of discourse, The spirit in my bosom, presseth me on.
text.KJV: 32:18 For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.
text.NABRE: For I am full of words; the spirit within me compels me.

text.NRSV: My heart is indeed like wine that has no vent; like new wineskins, it is ready to burst.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my bosom, is like wine not opened, Like new wine-skins, it will burst.
text.KJV: 32:19 Behold, my belly is as wine, which hath no vent, it is ready to burst like new bottles.
text.NABRE: My belly is like unopened wine, like wineskins ready to burst.

text.NRSV: I must speak, so that I may find relief; I must open my lips and answer.
text.Rotherham: I will speak, that I may freely breathe, I will open my lips and respond.
text.KJV: 32:20 I will speake, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lippes, and answere.
text.NABRE: Let me speak and obtain relief; let me open my lips, and reply.

text.NRSV: I will not show partiality to any person or use flattery toward anyone.
text.Rotherham: Let me be partial to no man, and, unto no son of earth, give flattering titles,
text.KJV: 32:21 Let me not, I pray you, accept any mans person: neither let me giue flattering titles vnto man.
text.NABRE: I would not be partial to anyone, nor give flattering titles to any.

text.NRSV: For I do not know how to flatter—or my Maker would soon take me away.”
text.Rotherham: Surely I know not how to give flattering titles, How soon might my Maker take me away!
text.KJV: 32:22 For I know not to giue flattering titles: in so doing my maker would soone take me away.
text.NABRE: For I know nothing of flattery; if I did, my Maker would soon take me away.

text.NRSV: “But now, hear my speech, O Job, and listen to all my words.
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, hear, I pray thee, Job, my discourse, and, to all my words, give thou ear.
text.KJV: 33:1 Wherefore, Iob, I pray thee, heare my speeches, and hearken to all my wordes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 33 Therefore, O Job, hear my discourse; listen to all my words.

text.NRSV: See, I open my mouth; the tongue in my mouth speaks.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I pray thee, I have opened my mouth, My tongue, with my palate, hath spoken,
text.KJV: 33:2 Behold, now I haue opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.
text.NABRE: Behold, now I open my mouth; my tongue and voice form words.

text.NRSV: My words declare the uprightness of my heart, and what my lips know they speak sincerely.
text.Rotherham: Mine utterances come straight from mine own heart, and, what I know, my lips have truly spoken;
text.KJV: 33:3 My words shalbe of the vprightnesse of my heart: and my lippes shall vtter knowledge clearely.
text.NABRE: I will state directly what is in my mind, my lips shall speak knowledge clearly;

text.NRSV: The spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
text.Rotherham: The spirit of GOD, hath made me, and, the inspiration of the Almighty, giveth me life.
text.KJV: 33:4 The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almightie hath giuen me life.
text.NABRE: For the spirit of God made me, the breath of the Almighty keeps me alive.

text.NRSV: Answer me, if you can; set your words in order before me; take your stand.
text.Rotherham: If thou art able to answer me, Set in order before me—take thy stand!
text.KJV: 33:5 If thou canst, answere me, set thy wordes in order before me, stand vp.
text.NABRE: If you are able, refute me; draw up your arguments and take your stand.

text.NRSV: See, before God I am as you are; I too was formed from a piece of clay.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I am like thyself toward GOD, From clay, have I been nipped off, even I!
text.KJV: 33:6 Behold, I am according to thy wish in Gods stead: I also am formed out of the clay.
text.NABRE: Look, I am like you before God, I too was pinched from clay.

text.NRSV: No fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy on you.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my terror, will not startle thee, nor, my hand, upon thee, be heavy.
